runners.name: Carry The Flag
runners.spotlight: Chased home Star Prospect at the Curragh (soft) in April but made odds-on backers sweat when just coming out the right side of a three-horse photo at Naas (also 5f, good) three weeks later; up to 6f when good second to Great Barrier Reef in Curragh Group 3 last time and that form looks all the better after his conqueror won the Coventry on Tuesday; drop back to this stiff 5f is unlikely to be an issue and he commands great respect from a stable that's won this four times, including last year.
runners.comment: Good second to Tuesday's Coventry winner latest; stiff 5f won't be an issue; big player

runners.name: Flight Signal
runners.spotlight: £180,000 breeze-up purchase who left an odds-on shot trailing when impressively taking five-runner Bath novice (5f, good to firm) on his debut four weeks ago; likely to build on that and he could be anything.
runners.comment: Impressive Bath win (5f, good to firm) on his debut four weeks ago; could be anything

runners.name: Where Love Lives
runners.spotlight: Favourite when making all at Thirsk on debut in April and stuck to his guns when winning Sandown's Listed National Stakes (also 5f, good; Mr Macartney an eye-catching sixth) three weeks ago; Sandown second ran a screamer in the Coventry on Tuesday; considered for a yard that's won this race before.
runners.comment: 2-2 and won Listed National Stakes three weeks ago; yard has won this before; player

runners.name: Ez Tina
runners.spotlight: Big filly who clocked a good speed figure when making all on Woodbine's Tapeta (5f) on her introduction in Canada last month; impressed quite a few observers that day and, alongside stablemate Through The Years, she's a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Big filly who clocked a good speed figure when making all on debut (AW) in Canada; player
runners.owner: Breeze Easy LLC & Resolution Road Stables

runners.name: Fanshell Beach
runners.spotlight: Hit the line strongly to make a winning debut over 4.5f on dirt at Churchill Downs in USA in April; trainer reportedly confident the filly will adapt to turf but jockey bookings might suggest she's third best of the yard's three-pronged attack.
runners.comment: Jockey bookings may suggest she's third best of trainer's three-pronged attack; turf debut

runners.name: Through The Years
runners.spotlight: Probably should have won on her debut at Keeneland in April and was long odds-on when making no mistake, breaking the track record in the process, at Aqueduct (also 5.5f, firm) last month; looks a typical speedball for a US yard that's won the Norfolk twice, including with this one's sire in 2013; one of three intriguing Wesley Ward fillies.
runners.comment: Broke the track record last time; fascinating contender from a yard that's won this twice
runners.owner: Magniers, Shanahan, RT Racing, Twin Oaks

runners.name: Persian Spring
runners.spotlight: Set the standard and didn't need to improve a great deal from his debut when taking 15-runner York novice (6f, good) last month; not far off the top of the list on RPRs and holds a Group 1 entry, so can't be passed over lightly in the hands of a trainer who won this in 2014.
runners.comment: Not far off the top of the list on RPRs and holds a Group 1 entry; not passed over lightly

runners.name: Savage Mariner
runners.spotlight: Finished runner-up to a 400,000gns breeze-up purchase at Leicester (6f, good to firm) 25 days ago; goes up in grade and down in trip for this second start and will need to step forward; a big market mover yesterday.
runners.comment: Up in grade and down in trip for this second start and will need to step forward

runners.name: Agamemnon
runners.spotlight: Big chunk of improvement on his second start when winning comfortably at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) last month; Oisin Murphy retains the ride and this return to turf is unlikely to pose a problem; draw on the wing is probably unhelpful but he can't be readily dismissed.
runners.comment: Big chunk of improvement to win second start; return to turf unlikely to pose a problem

runners.name: New Yorker
runners.spotlight: All starts at 5f; odds-on when winning on his debut at Dundalk (AW) in April but he let favourite backers down in a big way on his turf debut the following month and again could beat only one home at Naas (also good) a fortnight later; hood goes on.
runners.comment: Odds-on debut winner on AW but disappointing subsequently; hood goes on
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Social Symbol
runners.spotlight: £145,000 breeze-up purchase who defied some market weakness to go in first time at Ripon (5f, good); third has won since and William Buick takes over; yard won this in 2019 and he's one of a few interesting outsiders.
runners.comment: Won first time (5f, good); third won since and Buick takes over; outsider to consider

runners.name: Force Noir
runners.spotlight: Odds-on both starts; only just denied by Wednesday's Windsor Castle fourth on debut at the Curragh (heavy) in March and made no mistake when scooting in at Naas (also 5f, good) six weeks later; subsequently left Robson Aguiar (still in same ownership); first-time blinkers sound a note of caution on this first start since; otherwise respected.
runners.comment: Scooted up (5f, good) in April; blinkers a note of caution on stable debut, but respected

runners.name: Star Prospect
runners.spotlight: Produced a good burst to beat Carry The Flag and win going away at the Curragh (soft) on April introduction but could manage only an odds-on third at Naas (also 5f, good; raced freely) one month later; that race was a bit of a mess and there's a strong suspicion this one will set up better; halved in price yesterday (20-1 to 10-1); holds notable future entries and he's interesting from a yard that is having a fairytale week.
runners.comment: Good burst to beat Carry the Flag on debut; latest effort can be forgiven; interesting

runners.name: Blake's Monarch
runners.spotlight: Gave it a good go when just pegged at Wolverhampton (AW) on his debut but weakened to finish sixth at Chester (also 6f, good) 19 days later; drop to 5f might help but he has bundles to find.
runners.comment: Weakened to finish sixth at Chester (6f, good) latest; has bundles to find
runners.owner: The Good Racing Co Ltd & Partners

runners.name: Mr Macartney
runners.spotlight: Beat 11 others to pick up Leicester maiden (good to firm) in April on second start; only sixth of eight in a Listed event at Sandown (again 5f, good) last time but that doesn't tell the whole story, given he bungled the start before coming home well; runner-up that day filled the same position in Tuesday's Coventry; might be the sort to go well at wild odds; visor added.
runners.comment: Ran better than it looks in Listed event last time; may go well at wild odds; visor added

runners.name: Sovereign Glory
runners.spotlight: Built on encouraging debut when off the mark at Bath (5f, firm) last month; that bare form will need improving on.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Bath (5f, firm) last month; that bare form will need improving on

runners.name: El Floridita
runners.spotlight: Attracted some support at big odds on his May debut and proved those nibbles were on the right lines when bagging a Redcar maiden (5f, good to firm) nine days later; this is a radically different test, though; sold for £100,000 on Monday.
runners.comment: Won Redcar maiden (5f, good to firm) but this is a different test; £100,000 buy on Monday

runners.name: Tribeca
runners.spotlight: Made most when scoring at Navan (6f, good to yielding) on his April debut; second and third have won since, as has the fourth, who went in at 33-1 in the Windsor Castle on Wednesday; sire Blue Point couldn't get enough of this place and there's plenty to like about this son.
runners.comment: Form of his debut win could hardly be working out better; plenty to like
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Mrs A O'Callaghan

runners.name: Orthodox
runners.spotlight: Warm in the market before he won going away at Salisbury (5f, good) on his debut last month; mixed messages on that form but his respected yard won this race in 2012 and he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Won (5f, good) on debut last month; mixed messages on form but he's open to improvement

runners.name: Mussab
runners.spotlight: Stable has fond memories of this race, having won it in 2021 and 2022, and this colt recorded an encouraging first go when third at York (6f, good) four weeks ago; that bare form leaves him with work to do but this drop to 5f is unlikely to pose a problem.
runners.comment: Bare form of debut leaves him with work to do but drop to 5f is unlikely to pose a problem

runners.name: Underdog
runners.spotlight: Hails from a yard in ripping form and he played his part when making a winning debut, despite having to run with one of his shoes missing, at Sandown (5f, good) just eight days ago; coincidentally his sire Perfect Power won this race in 2021 having also scored eight days beforehand; a likely outsider who needs considering.
runners.comment: Yard in fine form; won recent debut despite running without a shoe; outsider to consider

runners.name: Jan Brueghel
runners.spotlight: Dual Group 1 winner who has a record of 6-9 overall and had a mitigating factor (set an unsuitably steady pace) in his 2025 King George defeat in sole C&D attempt; played a distant second fiddle last time when bidding for a Coronation Cup double at Epsom but nothing behind the winner showed their form in the rain-soaked conditions; the peak form he showed when beating Calandagan 12 months earlier gives him a big chance in this field.
runners.comment: Has a big chance on peak form, namely a defeat of Calandagan in the 2025 Coronation Cup
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Kalpana
runners.spotlight: Very smart mare who defied a penalty in Group 3 at Newbury (1m4f; beat West Wind Blows by a neck) on seasonal debut, taking record since wearing cheekpieces to 2-3 and running right up to her best; strong record at Ascot features wins in the British Champions Fillies & Mares Stakes for the last two years (soft/good) and an excellent second in the 2025 King George; avoids a Group 1 penalty in this race and has a major chance at the weights.
runners.comment: Has a strong record at Ascot and avoids a Group 1 penalty; major chance at the weights

runners.name: Santorini Star
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; broadly progressive and versatile mare whose strike-rate is 7-13; last four performances (1m4f-1m7f) were two wins at Group 2 level and second-place finishes in Group 1 races, beaten only narrowly at Longchamp most recently; currently falls short of the standard set by the other female in this field but has a trajectory that bodes well; wears first-time headgear; adds interest.
runners.comment: Broadly progressive mare; dual Group 2 winner; wears first-time headgear; adds interest

runners.name: Goliath
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground; has shown very smart form in various parts of the world, most recently prevailing in Group 2 contest in France where he is trained; record over C&D comprises a creditable second in this race and easy success (recorded peak RPR) in the King George, both in 2024; gained his other Group 1 win on German soil last season; strong contender on belated return to this venue.
runners.comment: Easily won the King George in 2024 after finishing second in this race; strong contender
runners.owner: Resolute Bloodstock & Philip Baron Von Ullmann

runners.name: Lambourn
runners.spotlight: Winner of the 2025 Derby; has worn cheekpieces this season, gamely beating Bay City Roller in the Huxley at Chester (good-ground Group 2) then failing by a long way to confirm the placings in a one-sided Coronation Cup on softer going; behind stablemate Jan Brueghel in that contest and is still a tad inferior to that horse on ratings but it will be interesting to see what he can do in the retained headgear returned to a forecast faster surface.
runners.comment: Interesting to see what he can do in retained cheekpieces back on forecast faster ground
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Giavellotto
runners.spotlight: Has finished in front of Kalpana twice but that rival had excuses both times; far from certain to confirm the placings but he's still one of the main players on the figures back down in grade; his Group 1 form features a success and creditable second in the last two runnings of the Hong Kong Vase at Sha Tin (1m4f, good); unsuited by the way the race panned out at Meydan latest start.
runners.comment: Excusable run last time; in front of Kalpana twice but that rival had excuses both times
runners.owner: Scuderia La Tesa Limited & Vaibhav Shah

runners.name: Best Secret
runners.spotlight: Versatile as regards ground; French colt whose form on British soil comprises a good third at Royal Ascot and success at Glorious Goodwood last term, both in 1m2f handicaps; beat West Wind Blows by 3l in Group 3 at Longchamp most recently, making it four wins from his last six starts and taking record over 1m4f to 2-2; progressive and may well rate higher still, especially at this trip; faces hotter company upped to this big stage but warrants respect the way he's going.
runners.comment: Successful in four of last six starts and is 2-2 over 1m4f; may well rate higher still

runners.name: Ethical Diamond
runners.spotlight: Ploughed a remarkable path last year, having gone from running well at the Cheltenham Festival to completing a prestigious Flat hat-trick (1m4f/1m6f, firm/good to firm) that comprised wins in the Duke of Edinburgh Stakes at Royal Ascot, Ebor at York and Breeders' Cup Turf at Del Mar; best to forgive his below-par effort behind two of today's rivals at Meydan in sole run this term (race panned out unfavourably); notable contender, with his excellent 2025 progress still in mind.
runners.comment: Completed a prestigious hat-trick last year; sole race this term didn't pan out favourably

runners.name: West Wind Blows
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a weaker edition of this contest in 2023; benefited from good tactical ride when almost certainly flattered by his proximity to Calandagan in Meydan Group 1 in March and received 4lb when only a neck behind Kalpana at Newbury most recently; worse off with that mare and has never won above Group 3 level but is otherwise a consistent sort who should again give his running.
runners.comment: Worse off with Kalpana on Newbury form but consistent and should give his running

runners.name: Amiloc
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground slower than good to yielding; disappointing favourite in the Yorkshire Cup on reappearance but that's his only blip in an otherwise progressive career; justified favouritism in the King Edward VII Stakes over C&D at this meeting (good to firm) 12 months ago, taking record at that time to 5-5, then ran well in the Irish St Leger and Breeders' Cup Turf; possibilities, provided his upward trajectory is resumed.
runners.comment: Disappointing on reappearance; progressive otherwise and won at this meeting last year

runners.name: Sons And Lovers
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent performer who wasn't disgraced on bare figures at Chester (1m5f) last time but finished almost 8l behind Jan Brueghel, getting nowhere near to defying a penalty for his Group 2 Saudi handicap win (1m7f); faces a stiff task and needs help from first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Generally consistent but faces a stiff task and needs help from first-time headgear

runners.name: Phantom Flight
runners.spotlight: Enhanced his good strike-rate in Bahrain with Group 3 success in April; weakened into last of six (well below par) at Newbury since and it's nearly two years since he last prevailed on home soil; doubts are cemented by this difficult assignment back up in grade.
runners.comment: Has a particularly good strike-rate in Bahrain; difficult assignment in this field

runners.name: Sajir
runners.spotlight: Won the Group 1 Prix Maurice de Gheest at Deauville (6.5f, good) last August; kicked off this season with two odds-on Listed defeats but won Group 3 at Chantilly (5f, good) latest; could be in the mix if at the top of his game.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner last August and landed Group 3 at Chantilly last time; might be in the mix

runners.name: Almeraq
runners.spotlight: Very impressive on handicap debut at Ayr (6f, good to soft) last July before a nasty fall at York, and it was a welcome sight when he reappeared with a Listed win at Salisbury (6f, good); below par on sole run on fast ground and others have more substance to their form, but he's an exciting prospect who could still have plenty more to offer for his top stable.
runners.comment: Returned with Listed win; others have more substance to form but he's an exciting prospect

runners.name: Lake Forest
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup over C&D (good to firm) at this meeting in 2024; campaigned over 7f-1m since that November; reappeared at Haydock (7f, good) last month having been gelded and powered clear for impressive Listed win; the drop back to 6f poses a question but the stiff track and strong pace will be in his favour, and he could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Impressive 7f Listed win last month; stiff track and strong pace will help back at 6f

runners.name: Joliestar
runners.spotlight: Five Group 1 wins in Australia, including on her last two starts (6.5f/6f, good/soft) this spring; top Australian sprinters tend to be a notch ahead of their European counterparts and she could take plenty of beating.
runners.comment: Five Group 1 wins in Australia, two this spring, and she could take plenty of beating

runners.name: Kind Of Blue
runners.spotlight: Fourth in the Commonwealth Cup here (good to firm) during progressive 2024 campaign, which culminated in Group 1 C&D win (soft) in the British Champions Sprint; 0-6 since but runner-up in the Group 1 Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good) last September and filled the same position on last month's return in the Group 2 Minster Stakes at York (6f, good); may not be far away.
runners.comment: C&D Group 1 win in October 2024; 0-6 since but with some strong form; may not be far away

runners.name: Satono Reve
runners.spotlight: Two-time Japanese Grade 1 winner who was a clear second in this race last year (good to firm); he's taking the same route as last season, again arriving after finishing second to the Hong Kong phenomenon Ka Ying Rising in the Group 1 Chairman's Sprint Prize at Sha Tin in April; key player.
runners.comment: Two-time Japanese Grade 1 winner; clear second in this race last year; key player

runners.name: Comanche Brave
runners.spotlight: The drop back to 6f proved to be a good move this spring, with a very respectable fifth in a hot Group 1 at Sha Tin in April (good to firm; Satono Reve second) and a 2l win in the Group 2 Greenlands Stakes at the Curragh (6f, good); not fully exposed at the trip and he's one to be interested in.
runners.comment: The drop back to 6f proved to be a good move this spring; won Group 2 by 2l; interesting

runners.name: Aramram
runners.spotlight: Last season concluded with C&D handicap win (soft); better than ever this year with a Listed win (6f, good to soft) and Group 3 second (6f, good) and another solid run when fifth of 13 in Group 2 at York (6f, good) last time; however, his form needs to go to another new level today.
runners.comment: Listed win and Group 3 near-miss this season, but form needs to go to another new level

runners.name: Flora Of Bermuda
runners.spotlight: Third in this race last year (good to firm) and also in the Group 1 Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good) in September; reappeared with Listed win at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last month and that form has been franked by the runner-up Rosy Affair; each-way player.
runners.comment: Third in this race last year and reappeared with Listed win at Newmarket; each-way player

runners.name: Overpass
runners.spotlight: 0-8 since winning Australian Group 1 (6f, good) in November 2024 but he's run some mighty races in the meantime, most recently third in the Group 1 King Charles III Stakes here (5f, good to firm) on Tuesday when faring best of those who raced prominently; that was a first run over a bare 5f since 2021 and it's possible that 6f is his optimum trip; he has to be respected.
runners.comment: Third in 5f King Charles III here on Tuesday and it's possible that 6f is his optimum trip

runners.name: Powerful Glory
runners.spotlight: Won the Group 2 Mill Reef Stakes at Newbury (6f, heavy) in 2024 on final 2yo start and landed 200-1 shock in the British Champions Sprint over this C&D (good) last October; down the field at the Curragh on last month's reappearance and he needs to prove the form he showed for his Group 1 win was no one-off, but it would be folly to dismiss his claims.
runners.comment: Needs to prove his 200-1 C&D Group 1 win was no one-off, but folly to dismiss his claims

runners.name: Time For Sandals
runners.spotlight: 25-1 winner of the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup over C&D (good to firm) at this meeting last year and she showed that was no fluke when running well at Glorious Goodwood next time; however, not at the same level since and down the field in the King Charles III Stakes over 5f here on Tuesday.
runners.comment: Last year's Commonwealth Cup winner but she finished down the field here on Tuesday
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Sayidah Dariyan
runners.spotlight: Close fourth in the Commonwealth Cup over C&D (good to firm) 12 months ago and readily won the Group 3 Summer Stakes against fellow fillies at York (6f, good) last July; last month's comeback third to Flora Of Bermuda at Newmarket gives her something to build on but others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Fourth in last year's Commonwealth Cup then won a Group 3, but others are more compelling

runners.name: Stolen Kiss
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has shown improved form this year, winning two Listed races in France (5.5f/6f, good to soft) with subsequent Group 3 winner Sajir in second each time; however, today's company demands another step forward.
runners.comment: Two Listed wins in France this year but today's company demands another step forward

runners.name: Great Wish
runners.spotlight: Danish-trained 5yo who won a Listed race at Meydan (6f, good) in January and performed respectably in defeat in Group 3 and Group 1 races there subsequently; however, it's likely he'll again come up short this afternoon.
runners.comment: Listed win at Meydan in January; beaten in Group races there since; set to come up short
runners.owner: The Foundation Of Lone & Kaj Nielsen

runners.name: Regional
runners.spotlight: 0-9 since winning the Group 1 Sprint Cup at Haydock (6f, good) in September 2023 but he's often run extremely well in the meantime, finishing placed in the 2024 and 2025 runnings of the King Charles III Stakes here (5f, good to firm) and third on last month's reappearance in the Group 2 Minster Stakes at York (6f, good); each-way contender at a track he obviously relishes.
runners.comment: Placed in 2024 and 2025 runnings of the King Charles III Stakes here; each-way contender

runners.name: Lugal
runners.spotlight: 6f Grade 1 winner in Japan (good to firm) and beaten just a neck in the Group 1 Al Quoz Sprint at Meydan (6f, good) in March when last seen; this is a hotter Group 1 but he could be an each-way contender.
runners.comment: Went close in the Group 1 Al Quoz at Meydan; this is hotter, but each-way possibilities

runners.name: Khaadem
runners.spotlight: 10yo who is very hard to fancy on what he's produced on his last three starts, down the field in the Temple Stakes at Haydock four weeks ago; however, he showed considerable spark as recently as last October, with a Grade 2 win in the US, and he won this race in 2023 (at 80-1) and 2024 (14-1); didn't run in it last year; certainly not written off today.
runners.comment: 10yo; big question mark about current form but he won this race in 2023 and 2024

runners.name: Jasour
runners.spotlight: Third in the Group 1 Commonwealth Cup over C&D (good to firm) at this meeting in 2024; however, he's failed to win since; opposable once more.
runners.comment: Third in Commonwealth Cup at this meeting in 2024 but he's failed to win since

runners.name: Catullus
runners.spotlight: Disappointing when favourite at Meydan in February, but returned from wind surgery and a gelding operation with a much improved effort to win 17-runner handicap at Goodwood (7f, good) with stacks in hand; that looks solid form and a repeat would give him major claims for a stable that won this race in 2021 and 2022.
runners.comment: Returned from wind/gelding operations with striking Goodwood win; major claims up in grade

runners.name: Saber Strike
runners.spotlight: Highly promising colt who cost 230,000gns as a yearling and came from last to first when beating five rivals in Listed event at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) on reappearance, maintaining his unbeaten record; high in the pecking order on ratings after just two outings and has lots of potential; could be anything.
runners.comment: Highly promising; maintained unbeaten record in Newmarket Listed race; could be anything

runners.name: Neolithic
runners.spotlight: Has improved on his 2yo efforts this term, winning a conditions race at Gowran (7f, soft; unraced on firmer than good) in between thirds in Listed races at the Curragh; solid chance on the figures and he's Ryan Moore's pick; wears blinkers for the first time.
runners.comment: Has improved on 2yo efforts this term; solid chance on ratings and he's Ryan Moore's pick
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: The Prettiest Star
runners.spotlight: Went down narrowly in Group 2 Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) on second 2yo start and ran at least as well when fourth in the 1,000 Guineas there (1m, good to firm) on reappearance; should cope with today's drop back in distance and it's unlikely she's reached her ceiling just yet; leading contender for last year's winning stable.
runners.comment: Fine fourth in 1,000 Guineas and should cope with this drop back in trip; big player

runners.name: Avicenna
runners.spotlight: Progressed well in his first three outings, with 7f 2yo wins at Doncaster (good to firm/good) and a promising second in the Craven (raced freely, bit short of room), but he ran no sort of race in the 2,000 Guineas (last of 14; 14-1) seven weeks ago; still low-mileage and could bounce back.
runners.comment: Had been progressing prior to finishing last in 2,000 Guineas; could bounce back

runners.name: America Queen
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced filly; made an encouraging return when beaten a neck in Group 3 Nell Gwyn at Newmarket (7f, good) in April; late withdrawal from Irish 1,000 Guineas on veterinary advice; remains open to improvement and one of the likelier candidates.
runners.comment: Lightly raced filly, beaten neck in Nell Gwyn at Newmarket; one of the likelier candidates

runners.name: Thesecretadversary
runners.spotlight: Chesham Stakes runner-up here in 2025; convincing winner of Leopardstown Group 3 (7f, good to yielding) on reappearance before racing freely in 2,000 Guineas and Irish 2,000 Guineas; has had more racing than most of these and has to carry a penalty but should be suited by this return to 7f.
runners.comment: Group 3 winner before racing freely in 2,000 Guineas/Irish 2,000; return to 7f should suit
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Morris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Developed into a useful 2yo, following a close second in the Group 2 Vintage at Goodwood (7f, good) by beating his two rivals in a Salisbury Listed race (1m, good to firm); has a lengthy absence to overcome, and this drop back in distance isn't an obvious plus judged on his pedigree, but he's in excellent hands and retains potential.
runners.comment: Ended 2yo season with Salisbury Listed win; has absence to overcome but retains potential

runners.name: Colori Forever
runners.spotlight: Tends to be delivered late; keeps getting better and showed his liking for this C&D when beating nine opponents with plenty in hand in handicap here six weeks ago; should continue on an upward curve and he's well worth his place in this higher grade.
runners.comment: Keeps improving; C&D handicap winner last time; rise in class looks fully justified

runners.name: Andab
runners.spotlight: Consistent gelding whose third to the high-class Talk Of New York in Sandown Listed race (1m, good) last time was just the latest in a string of creditable efforts; should be fine back at 7f but he's beginning to look exposed and the first-time visor (replacing cheekpieces) needs to give him a lift.
runners.comment: Consistent but exposed; visor (replacing cheekpieces) needs  to give him a lift

runners.name: Into The Sky
runners.spotlight: Second in the 6f Group 2 Mill Reef Stakes as a 2yo; shaped really well when fourth in the 2,000 Guineas at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) on return, fading after disputing lead until over 1f out; that form has worked out notably well and this intermediate distance should suit him down to the ground; strong contender.
runners.comment: Shaped really well when fourth in the 2,000 Guineas and this drop in trip should be ideal
runners.owner: Mrs D Tabor, Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

runners.name: Take Charge Star
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 7f maiden at Gowran last September; seemed to excel himself when eighth in the Irish 2,000 Guineas four weeks ago (50-1; wore tongue-strap) despite beating just one rival; one of the outsiders again.
runners.comment: Beat just one rival when eighth in Irish 2,000 Guineas; one of the outsiders again

runners.name: Dorset
runners.spotlight: Capped a productive 2yo campaign by winning the valuable Goffs Million at the Curragh and a Group 3 at Leopardstown, both on softish ground (unraced on firmer than good); no better than seventh this year, facing a stiff task in the French 2,000 Guineas on latest; penalised and needs a personal best.
runners.comment: Had a productive 2yo campaign but no better than seventh this year and carries a penalty

runners.name: Green Sense
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for £700,000 on Monday; won Group 2 Prix Robert Papin at Chantilly (6f, soft) last summer; wasn't disgraced when sixth of eight in the same grade there on reappearance, fading on first go at 1m (good ground); this return to 7f should help but others have stronger claims on form.
runners.comment: Group 2 winner as 2yo; had excuse on reappearance but others have stronger claims on form

runners.name: Domina Ignis
runners.spotlight: 1-1 as 2yo; shaped well on return when third in Group 3 Fred Darling at Newbury (7f, good); however, she ran no sort of race in the 1,000 Guineas (66-1) and is on a retrieval mission, with plenty to find on the figures.
runners.comment: Third in Group 3 on reappearance but ran no race in 1,000 Guineas; on a retrieval mission

runners.name: Ardisia
runners.spotlight: Five wins in a stellar 2025 included the Redcar Two Year Old Trophy, but his form has plateaued this season; ran respectably in Listed race at Epsom on Oaks day but he'll need much more to figure here.
runners.comment: Five wins as 2yo but form has plateaued; others make more appeal

runners.name: Billecart
runners.spotlight: Looks ready for this return to 7f after racing freely in Listed race at Newcastle and 2,000 Guineas at Newmarket (66-1), but he has a mountain to climb on the figures and is difficult to recommend.
runners.comment: This return to 7f may help but he has a mountain to climb on the figures

runners.name: Binhareer
runners.spotlight: Respectable C&D form last September when third of 19 but he's been at his most impressive on flatter tracks so far, as when crushing his rivals at Ayr (good to soft) and York (good) as a 3yo; returned to action with a mighty effort on the latter track in May, when only just failing to reel in a well-treated front-runner; major chance if reproducing the form back here, despite going up 5lb since, as his star is still rising.
runners.comment: Two impressive 6f wins as 3yo; smart form when runner-up on return; up 5lb; star is rising

runners.name: Hammer The Hammer
runners.spotlight: Up-and-coming handicapper last summer when winning first two starts at this level before finishing an excellent second of 24 in the 5f handicap here (good to firm) last June; first home of the 3yos when fifth in the Ayr Gold Cup (soft) last September and may do better this year; not ruled out now fitted with a tongue-tie on second run back.
runners.comment: Excellent 2nd in the 5f handicap here last June; first home of 3yos in Ayr Gold Cup since

runners.name: Realign
runners.spotlight: Made favourite but lacked experience when tackling the 5f handicap here last June; better form when winning at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last September and, after a low-key reappearance at Newmarket, he impressed when winning at Carlisle (6f, good to firm; first-time cheekpieces) recently; 1lb well in under a penalty; looks a smart prospect.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo who impressed when winning at Carlisle recently; looks a smart prospect

runners.name: Completely Random
runners.spotlight: Best form when dead-heating for 6f Listed AW win in January, making it 2-2 for Ryan Moore; no serious inroads back on turf since, in a Group 3 and the 7f Victoria Cup on this course, but he did come from off the pace to finish a close fifth of 28 in this race (good to firm) last year and he shouldn't be overlooked back over C&D in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: No serious inroads on turf of late but keeping-on 5th here last June; 2-2 for Ryan Moore

runners.name: Fandom
runners.spotlight: Exotic career includes races in the US, Britain, Ireland and on snow for various yards, but he comes here in fine form over 6f for this one, having finished close up at York (good; under 2l behind runner-up Binhareer) before winning at Thirsk; 1lb badly in under a penalty and has a harder task.
runners.comment: Exotic career for other yards; in good form for this one; 5lb penalty for recent 6f win

runners.name: Ten Pounds
runners.spotlight: All races over 7f until running a screamer in a close third of 28 (good to firm) in this race last year; ready winner of his next 6f start, on the AW last October; has had some trip and aids switches for new yard but looked back on a better path when a close, staying-on second in a 5f Carlisle Listed race (first-time cheekpieces replaced regular tongue-tie) last time; 3lb well in and has a big run in him back over C&D.
runners.comment: Close third here last June on first 6f run; best form for new yard latest; 3lb well in

runners.name: Flash Harry
runners.spotlight: Hooded all starts for this yard; can lead; has had just three runs in 6f handicaps, improving on a respectable effort in the Shergar Cup here when a ready winner at Goodwood (good to firm) last August; not so good on soft ground in the Ayr Gold Cup after and, while he is likely to do better as a 4yo, he was well held in a Listed contest on his comeback.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; ready winner at Goodwood (6f) in 2025; potential as 4yo but modest return

runners.name: Far Above Dream
runners.spotlight: Three of his last four wins have come at Goodwood (6f; heavy and good ground); probably not too track dependent as he won at Newbury in May but he has more to find on his first run at Ascot, off another career-high mark in a daunting race.
runners.comment: Newbury winner over 6f in May; on another career-high mark at a much more daunting level

runners.name: Double Rush
runners.spotlight: The talking horse in good 6f handicaps this spring and he delivered, coming clear with no fuss under Oisin Murphy at Newmarket (good) on yard debut and not having to work much harder under a penalty there (good to firm) in May for Shane Foley; 10lb higher than last time but could be on his way to better things.
runners.comment: Ready winner of two 6f races for new yard; may well defy 10lb higher mark than for latest

runners.name: Evening Saigon
runners.spotlight: Missed over a year of his career but returned to action twice since with 6f AW wins, last October and in April; well beaten on heavy in between but fared better at Goodwood (6f, good) in May when going down narrowly to Far Above Dream; has potential but others have more solid form; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Two AW wins since an absence; close call at Goodwood latest; others look more solid

runners.name: Dubai Bling
runners.spotlight: Improved as 4yo, narrowly winning the most recent of five starts in Bahrain (6f) and returning to Britain with a somewhat luckless close second at Chester (5f, good) in May to a stablemate; up a total of 10lb for those runs, though, and has much more to prove in this contest.
runners.comment: Improving; won final start in Bahrain before luckless 2nd over 5f; more to prove here

runners.name: Fast Track Harry
runners.spotlight: Won two AW races over 6f in early spring but he's had shortcomings in turf handicaps off marks in the 90s before and since, including over C&D, and looks to have his share of weight.
runners.comment: Two AW wins at 6f in early spring; weighted to best on turf form either side

runners.name: Sondad
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on good to firm last May; classy handicap form when runner-up in the Ayr Silver Cup (6f, soft) last September when first home on his side, which wasn't the first time he's had a difficult track position; carries a penalty for recent Epsom win (6f, soft; his second win in that race) but no surprise if he does better again as a 5yo; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner on good to firm; very best form on soft; penalised for Epsom win but respected

runners.name: Trefor
runners.spotlight: Useful form at a less competitive level in 2025, following 6f wins last June with one in a valuable York race (extended 5f, good to firm) in August; kept on well after being slightly checked in his run at Windsor in May but he has more to prove over this stiff 6f on course debut.
runners.comment: Three sprint wins 2025; this race is more competitive than he's used to; course debut
runners.owner: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner

runners.name: Soldier's Tree
runners.spotlight: Has had just four runs, winning a 7f AW maiden at Dundalk on second start; looked up against it in a 6f Listed race at Salisbury (good; first run without a hood) in May for new yard, but he gave a spirited show, taking a strong hold early and suffering serious interference before running on strongly to take a late close third, just behind Spy Chief; this strong pace can suit on handicap debut; intriguing.
runners.comment: Just four runs, winning on AW and useful 6f Listed form on recent yard debut; intriguing

runners.name: Caburn
runners.spotlight: Useful as 2yo sprinter; successful when sights lowered at Yarmouth last September and his third in a 6f Listed race at Doncaster (good to soft) in March was his best form; good to firm ground given as an excuse for a weak effort at Haydock in April, though, which doesn't augur well on today's surface.
runners.comment: Best run at 6f on good to soft; good to firm said to have been against him when held since

runners.name: Heathcliff
runners.spotlight: Took a step forward on the AW earlier in the year, at 7f and 6f, but he didn't make his turf debut until never involved in a Listed race at Haydock (7f, good) in May; comes from a line of superb turf sprinters but this is a difficult first handicap on grass, to put it mildly.
runners.comment: Improved AW form in 2026; held on turf debut; tough task in first handicap on grass

runners.name: Roman Dragon
runners.spotlight: Has won in Bahrain but all eight British wins have come at Chester and he's been soundly beaten in the last two runnings of this race (100-1 each time); has been wearing cheekpieces but visor is now refitted.
runners.comment: Chester specialist; soundly beaten in the last two runnings of this, 100-1 each time

runners.name: Royal Zabeel
runners.spotlight: A strike-rate of 4-7 on AW includes two Listed wins, at 7f and 6f; not quite so good on grass as yet but he has a 7lb lower turf mark and indicated he could make it pay when an excellent fifth in the big-field Victoria Cup over 7f here (good; has won on good to firm) in May, not ideally positioned there as it turned out; one to consider back at 6f.
runners.comment: 4-7 on AW; good 5th in big 7f race here in May off lower turf mark; considered back at 6f

runners.name: Toyotomi
runners.spotlight: Listed winner in France; mostly rather vulnerable in handicaps for this yard, including over C&D in May, but he didn't get much of a run behind Fandom at Thirsk (good) next time and confirmed he was on the way back when leading near the line at Goodwood (also 6f, soft) two weeks ago; has quite a bit more to do under a penalty (3lb badly in).
runners.comment: Won Listed in France; best form in Britain when winning latest; more to do under penalty

runners.name: Spy Chief
runners.spotlight: Often leads; showed high-class form on good to firm ground last summer, runner-up in the Group 3 Jersey (7f) at this meeting, then involved in a tight finish for third place in the Group 1 July Cup at Newmarket (6f) despite racing away from the main action for much of the way; had some excuses later but he was back on track when a close second in a 6f Listed race at Salisbury recently; can go well from rail draw.
runners.comment: High-class form over 7f and 6f on good to firm in 2025; back on track on Salisbury return

runners.name: Thunderbear
runners.spotlight: Back on song when convincing winner of a 6f Listed event at Naas (yielding) last October; unable to get involved in two runs at that level in 2026 but he was badly checked back at Naas in April and in better form than the 5l defeat suggests; however, he has more to prove back on this track with good to firm ground quicker than ideal; regular cheekpieces are discarded.
runners.comment: Convincing 6f Listed winner (yielding) last October; good to firm is quicker than ideal

runners.name: Two Tribes
runners.spotlight: Outstripped his previous form when winning a valuable 7f handicap here (good; effective on good to firm) last July before winning the 6f Stewards' Cup at Goodwood (good to soft) by a healthy margin under a penalty next time; shade vulnerable once reassessed and a narrow win at Meydan in January wasn't enough to think he's well treated for this race; extra push needed.
runners.comment: Won valuable 7f race here and 6f Stewards' Cup last summer; needs extra off this mark

runners.name: Candy
runners.spotlight: All wins over 6f (on soft and good ground) in the autumn months; excelled himself when winning the Ayr Silver Cup (soft) last September but it seems unlikely he will reproduce that form on this belated return.
runners.comment: All wins at 6f (soft/good) in autumn months; unlikely to be primed on belated return

runners.name: Leovanni
runners.spotlight: Sterling effort to win the Group 2 Queen Mary (5f, good to firm) at this meeting as a 2yo, then ran to a similar level in another Group 2 at York (6f); useful efforts in 5f Listed races in just two runs as a 3yo and 4yo (tongue tied latest; had wind surgery since) but the suitability of this test has to be proved.
runners.comment: Won Group 2 (5f) here in 2024; lightly raced since; trip query on first 6f run since 2yo

runners.name: Apollo One
runners.spotlight: Has contested three of the last four runnings, doing best when runner-up in 2023 (good to firm; also won a Group 3 over C&D on soft); has been a fine stalwart in other top sprint handicaps, including when only just losing out in the 2024 Portland at Doncaster on sole attempt for Christophe Soumillon; it's hard to ignore one with his history at this level.
runners.comment: Very effective at this level; 2nd in 2023 from 3 attempts in this race; can't be ignored

runners.name: Run Boy Run
runners.spotlight: These big-field 6f handicaps suit him down to the ground, and having finished fourth in the Stewards' Cup at Goodwood (good to soft) and the Portland at Doncaster (extended 5f, good) he led home 24 rivals in the Ayr Gold Cup (soft) last September; mostly in good form at Meydan this year but he's 10lb higher than at Ayr which is a big burden to carry.
runners.comment: Well suited by big-field 6f handicaps; won the Ayr Gold Cup in September; now 10lb higher

runners.name: Mitbaahy
runners.spotlight: Classy in his prime when winning a Group 2 at the Curragh (6f, good; acts on good to firm) in May 2024; only intermittently raced since owing to physical issues and his latest comeback, at Doncaster in March, ended badly; however, he's had a second wind surgery since and is now tongue tied, so judgement suspended with his trainer bullish ahead of this first handicap run.
runners.comment: Won Group 2 in May 2024; lightly raced since; modest comeback; wind op since; tongue tied

runners.name: Princling
runners.spotlight: Reappeared at 1-4 with a ready novice win; 2-1 favourite when good fourth of 14 for the rearranged Silver Bowl at Carlisle (also 1m on good to firm) three weeks ago on handicap debut, making inroads too late but that experience of a hot handicap at right-handed track could stand him in good stead and, in accordance with the dam's side, 1m2f now looks well worth exploring.
runners.comment: Silver Bowl 4th at Carlisle (1m) could stand him in good stead and 1m2f is worth exploring

runners.name: Perisher
runners.spotlight: 11-8 favourite in cheekpieces when he won a maiden at Gowran Park (1m, soft) and conditions event at Naas (10.4f, good; unraced on firmer) on his last two starts; made all and put 4.5l between himself and his rivals over the last 2f on the latest outing; revised mark could still be favourable, including as he's not fully exposed at this trip.
runners.comment: 1m and 10.4f wins on last two starts, impressive making all when upped in trip last time

runners.name: Allegresse
runners.spotlight: Won from the front in two Kempton novices (1m, AW) as 2yo; beat just one rival in the course of three starts since, still showing useful form on reappearance (last of four in a 1m conditions stakes) but eased right down once pursuit was in vain at Epsom (1m2f, soft) two weeks ago on handicap debut; plenty to prove but at least stamina in the family suggests he should stay.
runners.comment: Plenty to prove after recent results but stamina in the family suggests he should stay

runners.name: Lost Boys
runners.spotlight: Won the last three of his five starts; this season they were 1m2f handicaps (good; unraced on firmer) at Sandown and Newbury, latterly in the London Gold Cup in which he travelled very strongly before squeezing through over 1f out and prevailing virtually on the nod against Sahara King, as the pair drew about 2l clear; these powerful owners have acquired both horses since; up 7lb but improving and capable of better.
runners.comment: Won 1m2f handicaps (good) at Sandown and Newbury, latter London Gold Cup from Sahara King

runners.name: Sahara King
runners.spotlight: AW winner in November on debut and, coming from the back, good form in defeat this season; no real threat to Evanesco in five-runner handicap at Newmarket (1m2f, good to firm) on penultimate start but better when launching a strong challenge for the London Gold Cup at Newbury (1m2f, good) last time, losing out narrowly in a duel with Lost Boys; both Newbury principals have since been snapped up by Wathnan Racing; up 6lb but cheekpieces go on (perhaps some hesitation in front last time) and he's one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Up 6lb after his close call in London Gold Cup; cheekpieces go on; one to take seriously

runners.name: Amadeus Mozart
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Curragh maiden last June in his only race as a 2yo; never dangerous in 1m2f Group 3 this May but useful form when second in small-field events over 1m2f/1m4f on starts either side (last time 7l behind Thursday's Listed second Endorsement); lightly raced but needs to show something new for a prominent finish on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lightly raced but needs to show something new for prominent finish on this handicap debut
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Spyce
runners.spotlight: Stays 1m2f and acts on good to firm; put firmly in his place when 20-1 for Group 3 Classic Trial at Sandown on reappearance but made late headway into fourth of ten off this mark to Lost Boys in the London Gold Cup at Newbury (1m2f, good) on handicap debut, despite racing freely in rear early on; could have more to give and a stronger pace today may suit.
runners.comment: Free early on but made late headway into fourth of ten to Lost Boys in the London Gold Cup

runners.name: Bayaann
runners.spotlight: He made a smooth start with 7f AW win in November and built substantially on that when second in a novice at Newmarket (1m, good) last month; didn't reproduce that form when having to work hard for his win at 1-4 in a novice at Windsor (1m, good to soft) 12 days ago but the major plus point for this handicap debut is that his performance and pedigree both shout that 1m2f will suit him much better.
runners.comment: Handicap debut; performance and pedigree shout that 1m2f will suit him much better than 1m

runners.name: Folk Pageant
runners.spotlight: Last of nine on handicap debut (1m, AW) in April but won her other four starts this season; they were 1m2f handicaps at Newmarket, Chester (good) and Epsom (soft) on last three occasions and she's seen those races out strongly; raised another 10lb since that attritional contest two weeks ago, and she's never raced on firmer than good, but it's hard to knock one who's been finding such improvement.
runners.comment: Won 1m2f handicaps at Newmarket, Chester (good) and Epsom (soft) on last three starts
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Echo Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied first time and improved when off the mark in eight-runner novice at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago, leading over 2f out and comfortably seeing off Thursday's tailed-off King George V longshot Blue Hercules; more to offer, which is needed on this handicap debut; non-runner (very stiff task) in Friday's 1m4f Group 2 here.
runners.comment: Improved to win an eight-runner novice at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago

runners.name: Balzac
runners.spotlight: AW novice winner who was third in 1m2f Listed race at Epsom and 11.6f Derby Trial at Lingfield (good to firm); same fate as many when tailed off in the Derby (1m4f, soft; 66-1) two weeks ago; improvement is also needed if he's to make a splash on this handicap debut, but return to 1m2f can help.
runners.comment: Handicap debut after creditable 3rd in Lingfield Derby Trial but tailed off in the Derby

runners.name: Harmonics
runners.spotlight: Made low-key start in April but a totally different proposition in his two races since, emerging best in a bunched finish for maiden at Southwell (1m, AW) and pulverising the opposition in a novice at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm); changed hands since; very useful prospect going into this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Pulverised the opposition in a Doncaster novice (1m2f, good to firm); very useful prospect

runners.name: Sunset On Leros
runners.spotlight: His win was in a 7f novice at Doncaster (good) last July; subsequent efforts include minor honours in a Listed race and two Italian Group 3s, latterly emerging in third of 14 with first-time cheekpieces in their version of the 2,000 Guineas (1m, good); that does not make him well handicapped today but his sire gives plenty of hope for 1m2f.
runners.comment: Third of 14 with first-time cheekpieces in Italian 2,000 Guineas (1m, good) last time

runners.name: Nil Bua Gan Dua
runners.spotlight: Unraced on firmer than good; pitched into Group 2 and Group 3 events for minor roles on the last two of his three outings as 2yo; it wasn't until second start this term that he got off the mark (made all over 10.4f at Roscommon) and he had to knuckle down seriously, but that even-money success from a stablemate (who was 13th in Thursday's King George V) brings improved form into this much harder task for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Even-money win at Roscommon (10.4f, good) was improved form; makes his handicap debut
runners.owner: A K Whelan & Reeves Thoroughbred Racing

runners.name: Bourbon Blues
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with creditable third of seven in 1m1f Listed race at Newmarket; short of room at a crucial stage in the London Gold Cup at Newbury (1m2f, good; 16-1) and basically given an easy time from over 1f out, so he's a good deal better than last of ten in that hot contest suggests; however, after 11 races, he is more exposed than most of today's rivals.
runners.comment: Shaped a good deal better than London Gold Cup result but he's comparatively exposed
runners.owner: M Hughes, M Kerr-Dineen And Partners

runners.name: Evanesco
runners.spotlight: Mixed bag as 2yo but improved form in two 1m2f Newmarket handicaps this term; had it all his own way up front in five-runner race last time (good to firm) but stretched clear impressively from over 1f out; 8lb higher in a much more competitive affair today, but runner-up Sahara King went close in the always-strong London Gold Cup next time; considered.
runners.comment: Bossed a five-runner race last time (1m2f, good to firm) but stretched clear impressively

runners.name: French Master
runners.spotlight: First-time blinkers when he was a very taking winner of last year's Copper Horse Stakes (1m6f, good to firm) at this meeting in which he came from well behind to win going away; gelded and, in no headgear, returned to that level of form when third in Group 2 at Newmarket (1m4f) on reappearance, only to then finish last of the five in new cheekpieces (retained) in the Group 3 Aston Park at Newbury; however, the first two home at Newbury were Group 1 horses, and on reflection it wasn't that bad a run; the burning question is whether he'll stay.
runners.comment: One of the class acts in the field, albeit with his accolades achieved over shorter trips

runners.name: Illinois
runners.spotlight: Finished runner-up in last year's Gold Cup here (good to firm) and second again in the Goodwood version (2m, good), only narrowly failing to concede 14lb to Scandinavia; crystal clear claims on these favourable terms if returning to that level of form but he wasn't at his best when bidding for a second Ormonde at Chester and ran abysmally in the recent Coronation Cup when under the pump from a worryingly early stage (unwise to blame the rain-hit ground when he has soft-ground form); hard to dismiss but in equal measure he's hard to trust.
runners.comment: Has the form to win this but his Coronation Cup run was worryingly laboured
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Le Destrier
runners.spotlight: Has form in Poland, France and Germany, beaten only 7l in a 1m4f Group 1 in the last-named country in 2023; restricted to only three runs in the meantime, the latest after an absence of 691 days when a closing third behind Scandinavia in a 1m6f Group 3 at Leopardstown last month (good; won on good to firm in Poland) on his stable debut; trainer did have him in the Gold Cup and, not only is he one of the classier acts in this field, he could be one of the strongest stayers judged on that latest effort.
runners.comment: Debut for this yard when close up behind Scandinavia; one of the classier acts in this

runners.name: A Piece Of Heaven
runners.spotlight: Has very low mileage for an 8yo and has Flat form figures of 221 since the tongue-tie went on; unraced over this far but he looks all about stamina, having finished second in an Irish Cesarewitch (2m1f) and one better in last month's Chester Cup (2m2f, good; has run well on faster); that latest race went like clockwork from his low stall but he seems very uncomplicated, and this was nominated as his next target straight after that win; big player.
runners.comment: Chester Cup winner who should stay okay and these terms are favourable

runners.name: Berkshire Sundance
runners.spotlight: Good strike-rate of 7-17 and he should have brighter prospects than some of staying this extreme trip  given he wasn't stopping at the end of the Chester Cup over an extended 2m2f last time; has raised his game in cheekpieces (fitted in only his last two races) and he could outrun his odds if coping okay with fast ground.
runners.comment: There's no knocking a strike-rate of 7-17 and he wasn't stopping in the Chester Cup (2m2f)
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Dallas Star
runners.spotlight: Group 3 winner over 1m2f at three; his losing run of 13 includes a fading fourth in this last year (good to firm); likely flattered by his close proximity to Scandinavia in a 1m6f Listed race in April and hasn't fared so well since; even if back on song, this is a tough place for front-runners.
runners.comment: Has become a hard horse to win with and this place is tough for front-runners

runners.name: Columbus
runners.spotlight: Three wins in France included a Group 3 over 1m7f in September 2024 and he was even better in defeat last year, finishing close up in a German Group 1 (1m4f, good to soft; has won on good; unraced on faster); after joining Willie Mullins for 400,000euros (then gelded) he was runner-up in the same Killarney novice hurdle (2m4f) which Sober won on his debut for Mullins en route to winning this last year; this 5yo is firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Classy Flat form; 2nd over hurdles at Killarney; similar profile to 2025 winner Sober

runners.name: Mr Hollywood
runners.spotlight: Like stablemate Columbus, he was a Group 3 winner on the Flat (1m2f, heavy; effective on good; never encountered quicker); bought for 80,000euros and gelded after winning over 1m4f in France last September; should pay his way under both codes for new connections, though he was 7.5l behind Columbus when both made their debuts for Willie Mullins over hurdles at Killarney last month.
runners.comment: 7.5l behind Columbus when both made their debuts for Willie Mullins over hurdles

runners.name: Maxi King
runners.spotlight: Likeable 6yo who handles any ground and can usually be relied upon to give his running, as was the case when a close second to A Piece Of Heaven in the Chester Cup (2m2f, good); Toby Moore's claim meant the winner was giving him 8lb that day, so he looks to have his work cut out to reverse those placings; can run well but probably not well enough.
runners.comment: Second to A Piece Of Heaven in the Chester Cup but these terms are much tougher

runners.name: Hipop De Loire
runners.spotlight: Has some back-class on the Flat but the wheels came off big-time over hurdles for Willie Mullins and, after moving on for 15,000gns, he was tailed off at 50-1 on yard debut at Goodwood (1m6f; blinkers returned).
runners.comment: After moving on for 15,000gns, he was tailed off at 50-1 on yard debut at Goodwood

runners.name: Carlton
runners.spotlight: All eight Flat wins have been on the AW, though he's won twice over hurdles at up to 2m4f; very capable at the right level but this isn't it; faces a mammoth task on all the figures.
runners.comment: Capable at the right level but this isn't it; faces a mammoth task on all the figures

runners.name: Aajej
runners.spotlight: Signed off for the Crisfords with a 1m6f win on the AW in a Class 4 handicap, in first-time blinkers; well held over 2m here on yard debut (tried cheekpieces) and this is highly ambitious on all the figures.
runners.comment: Just one win from nine starts and this is highly ambitious on all the figures

runners.name: Pivotal Days
runners.spotlight: 7f winner for previous yard but very minor form under both codes for this one and it would be the shock of all shocks if he won this.
runners.comment: Limited under both codes; it would be the shock of all shocks if he won this

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Vauntingly
runners.spotlight: Favourite for a Gowran maiden on debut; had plenty to do after breaking slowly and stayed on well to finish 4 3/4l fourth; stepped up from that when taking on previous winners over the same course and trip; dictated a modest pace and was ultimately no match for the smart Sparan Nua, now a Group 3 winner; excellent chance here; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Took on previous winners at Gowran, well beaten by a smart filly, excellent chance here

runners.name: Camelot Queen
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly ran once at two; decent effort on return, taking third in a 1m2f Leopardstown fillies' maiden after making much of the running; dam is by Pivotal, and a lack of stamina may have been the issue when she faded tamely in a 1m4f maiden at Cork last month.
runners.comment: Fair third in a 1m2f Leopardstown fillies' maiden, appeared not to stay 1m4f at Cork

runners.name: Solas Na Gealai
runners.spotlight: Big price first time out in a 1m4f maiden at the Curragh early this month; failed to make much impact and will probably need to gain more experience.
runners.comment: Big price first time out at the Curragh recently, likely to need more time and experience

runners.name: Guinelot
runners.spotlight: E75,000 yearling; third foal; half-sister to French 7f winner Brothers Of Lily's; dam French 1m winner, closely related to 6f 2yo winner Swordplay, half-sister to French 1m2f 2yo Group 1 winner Robin Of Navan, hooded for debut.
runners.comment: E75,000 yearling, half-sister to French 7f winner, decent pedigree, hooded for debut

runners.name: Anaverna
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister by Bungle Inthejungle to useful five-time winner Bear Profit; dam is a half-sister to UAE Group 2 winner Allybar; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister by Bungle Inthejungle to useful five-time winner Bear Profit; hooded for debut

runners.name: Wickedly Wootton
runners.spotlight: E360,000 yearling; fourth foal; Wootton Bassett filly out of a Galileo mare who won the 1m Group 2 Prix de Sandringham. Stayed on strongly into fourth from an unpromising position behind the Ribblesdale runner-up Johanna Walsh on debut at Leopardstown; should go close with normal improvement.
runners.comment: Stayed on strongly from an unpromising position on debut at Leopardstown, should go close
runners.owner: E Tynan & S I Weston & Haras d'Etreham & Riviera Equine

runners.name: Charm Of Venice
runners.spotlight: Made no impact on debut at Leopardstown last October (7f, yielding); better effort at Dundalk (1m) in January when fifth of 14; pedigree suggests she may be well suited by this longer trip.
runners.comment: Fair fifth over 1m at Dundalk, pedigree suggests she may be suited by this longer trip

runners.name: Donna Time
runners.spotlight: Left an impression she would improve from the run when introduced at Dundalk in January, beaten around 7l in seventh in what looked like an ordinary contest; took a step in the right direction when sixth of 12 over 1m2f at Leopardstown early this month.
runners.comment: Took a step in the right direction when sixth of 12 over 1m2f at Leopardstown, more needed

runners.name: My First Rose
runners.spotlight: Half-sister by Camelot to two winners for this stable, Red Vibernum and Pierre Royal; dam, by Dubawi, won two 1m Listed races and is from a smart middle-distance family; failed to make much impression in a 1m2f Leopardstown maiden first time out.
runners.comment: Good pedigree, failed to make much impression in a 1m2f Leopardstown maiden first time out

runners.name: Ruby Vega
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and never involved in a 7f maiden at this venue last month; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Slowly away and never involved in a 7f maiden at this venue last month, safe to rule out

runners.name: Churchfield Lady
runners.spotlight: Pedigree implies she might have a stamina issue over this trip; that view is endorsed by her debut run at Cork when she ran out of steam over a furlong down.
runners.comment: Ran out of steam over a furlong down over a similar trip first time out at Cork

runners.name: Altura
runners.spotlight: Well-related filly was unfancied on debut in a 1m2f maiden at Leopardstown two months ago and was well beaten; some allowance should be made for interference suffered around three furlongs down.
runners.comment: Unfancied on debut in a 1m2f maiden at Leopardstown two months ago and was well beaten

races.race_name: Belfast Live: Number 1 For News And Sport Handicap

runners.name: Teologia
runners.spotlight: Built on a good second in a Ballinrobe claimer (9.5f, good to yielding) to win a C&D maiden last month (good) in first time cheekpieces (retained); a big player for her handicap debut; holds an Irish Oaks entry.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner last time; big player for h'cap bow

runners.name: Connecteo
runners.spotlight: Best effort by far on handicap bow at Roscommon when stepped up markedly in trip for a 0.5l second (1m4f, yielding); up 3lb but in the mix again.
runners.comment: Best effort by far upped in trip on h'cap bow when second; in the mix again up 3lb

runners.name: Glamazon
runners.spotlight: Well exposed before Ballinrobe maiden win in May (9.5f, good to yielding); poor efforts back in handicap company the last twice, beaten 18l at Leopardstown last time (1m, soft to heavy).
runners.comment: Ballinrobe m'den winner has been poor in two h'cap starts since then.

runners.name: Dreoilin
runners.spotlight: Looked decent when taking the runner-up spot in back-to-back races on softer ground at Navan (1m2f, heavy) and the Curragh (1m, soft); too keen when disappointed on good ground at Leopardstown and 1m4f trip likely stretched her too; forecast rain in her favour and she's worth another go.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice on softer before disappoint on better last time; worth another go

runners.name: Amrum
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt disappointed in four maidens but made an immediate splash in handicap company at Gowran when winning by a head (9.5f, yielding), running on well; raised 7lb for that win but claimed reduces that and he's interesting with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Frankel colt disappointed in m'dens but won h'cap bow last time; interesting

runners.name: Roaming Coyote
runners.spotlight: Huge improvement for switch to handicaps at Gowran when runner-up despite getting caught short of room and needing to switch over an extended 1m1f (yielding), although winner bombed out since; in the mix off 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Huge improvement for switch to h'caps when runner-up; in the mix off 6lb higher

runners.name: Espritroyale
runners.spotlight: No show in two maidens for Sheila Lavery last year; okay run in a Curragh maiden for this yard but took a backward step in a Roscommon handicap next time; took a big step up in trip to this C&D last time when 6.75l behind Teologia; down 3lb.
runners.comment: Down 3lb after finishing 6.75l behind Teologia over C&D; others more likely

runners.name: Hassaniya
runners.spotlight: Placed in a barrier trial last year before fair debut run in a 7f Naas maiden; comeback run at the Curragh was a similar level; outpaced but kept on for fifth over 1m at Gowran last time (good to yielding); worth a look in the market on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Maiden runs were not without promise; worth a look in the market on h'cap bow

runners.name: Arizona Star
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in all four starts, 33l behind on handicap bow at Roscommon last time.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in all four starts inc' on h'cap bow latest

runners.name: Chapel Island
runners.spotlight: Juvenile for Mick Mulvany wasn't progressive; well-backed for stable debut on 7f Fairyhouse (good) return in May but never got involved; plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Moderate juvenile form and didn't justify market support on return; plenty to prove

runners.name: Captain Con
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden form and no improvement in opening two handicap starts; beaten 10l at Fairyhouse last time.
runners.comment: Poor in m'dems and no improvement in opening two h'caps

runners.name: The Holy Apostle
runners.spotlight: Standout 2yo effort when placed in a Killarney nursery (1m, good) off 68; quite exposed and his Limerick return was woeful,
runners.comment: Placed in a nursery last year but quite exposed and his Limerick return was woeful

runners.name: Aquilla Star
runners.spotlight: Well held in maidens but much improved on heavy ground at Galway (7f) on handicap bow in final start of 2025 when running on for second at 50-1; steps up in trip now for seasonal return.
runners.comment: Much improved on hvy ground for h'cap bow in Oct' when second; up in trip for return

runners.name: Copper Craft
runners.spotlight: Form to date suggests he's better on AW with placed finishes twice at Dundalk this year; returned to turf at Fairyhouse with a very moderate effort; blinkers on now but others preferred.
runners.comment: Better on AW; moderate effort back on turf last time; others preferred.

runners.name: Swift Charm
runners.spotlight: No wins from seven starts but definite signs of promise with third-place finishes last twice; ran on well at Ballinrobe over an extended 1m1f (good to yielding, 50-1); confirmed that wasn't a fluke with her run at Fairyhouse (1m2f, good); softer ground would be an unknown.
runners.comment: 0-7 but promising third-place finishes last twice; softer ground would be an unknown

runners.name: Onyeisi
runners.spotlight: First reserve; AW winner for Charlie Johnston; has improved with each run this season for new yard; took fourth of 14 at Fairyhouse last time.
runners.comment: Reserve; has improved with each run for new yard this term, fourth of 14 latest

runners.name: Marians Gal
runners.spotlight: 0-8 on turf and yet to trouble the judge; won an AW handicap at Dundalk over the winter (10.5f) off 46; consistent form in that sphere earlier in the year, and his return to turf at Leopardstown (1m5f, good) last time when seventh of 19 was not devoid of promise.
runners.comment: 0-8 on turf and yet to trouble the judge but latest seventh of 19 not devoid of promise

runners.name: Coul Dore
runners.spotlight: A newly applied hood appeared to work the oracle at Fairyhouse last time (1m2f, good) when running his best race by far from five starts, beaten just a neck; 4lb rise for that seems a little harsh but chance if the hood continues its magic.
runners.comment: Newly applied hood worked the oracle when he took a close second last time; chance

runners.name: Cleopatra's Needle
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose mare; shaped very nicely on last start in this sphere at Leopardstown in April (1m, good to yielding) when a 0.75l second of 20; sole win on the level came at this trip; chance.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely for second in April; sole win in this sphere came at this trip; chance.

runners.name: Rising Sky
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Dundalk in March off 56 (10.5f) when wearing this headgear for the first time; two turf runs since then have not been promising; others more likely.
runners.comment: AW winner in March but two turf runs since have not been promising; others more likely

runners.name: Albatala
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden is 0-8 on Flat but there was promise in her fourth of 15 back from over a year off at Fairyhouse earlier this month (3l behind Swift Charm); soft ground would be a plus and not ruled out if taking a step forward.
runners.comment: 0-8 on Flat but promise in her return from over a year off; not ruled out

runners.name: Maker's Mark
runners.spotlight: No sign of winning potential so far from seven runs; beaten 28l on Fairyhouse return.
runners.comment: No sign of winning potential so far from seven runs; beaten 28l on Fairyhouse return
runners.owner: Hugh J O'Brien & Colm O'Brien & Ms Denise McCourt

runners.name: Slaney Swagger
runners.spotlight: 20-race maiden on turf but placed ten times, the latest when third at Thurles over 1m on good to yielding in October; off since poor effort at Dundalk in December; could go well on stable debut.
runners.comment: 20-race m'den on turf but placed ten times; could go well on stable debut

runners.name: Simpson's Paradox
runners.spotlight: Won a heavy ground Killarney maiden in 2023; moderate efforts in three outings this season; 6.25l eighth of 14 at Gowran last time (1m, yielding) when running on late.
runners.comment: Won a hvy ground m'den in 2023; moderate efforts in three outings this season

runners.name: Kilgharrahs Love
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; 0-15 and form this season doesn't provide much hope of a breakthrough.
runners.comment: Reserve; 0-15 and form this season doesn't provide much hope of a breakthrough

runners.name: Folk Warrior
runners.spotlight: Showed little in first six starts on the Flat and over hurdles; plugged on reasonably well when fifth over 1m4f at Dundalk in May but hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Plugged on for fifth on AW last time but hard to recommend on overall form

runners.name: Hosanna Power
runners.spotlight: Won a Newbury handicap for Sir Michael Stoute in 2024 off 81 (1m2f, good); form this season doesn't suggest he's on the verge of doubling up his tally
runners.comment: 2024 Newbury winner; Form this term doesn't suggest he's on verge of doubling up his tally

runners.name: Thistle Cross
runners.spotlight: No improvement over moderate maiden form for switch to handicaps over 7f here last time (good); hood on but best watched.
runners.comment: No improvement for switch to h'caps here last time; hood on but best watched

runners.name: Stonyfalls
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens; 66-1 for handicap debut when sixth of 14 here over 7f (good) on seasonal return; could improve; declared for 8.30 here on Friday.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in m'dens; 66-1 on h'cap bow when sixth of 14; could improve

runners.name: Talking Tough
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; not easy to win with as his record of 2-64 suggests; has beaten precious few rivals last season and this one
runners.comment: Reserve; 2-64 and beaten precious few rivals this and last season

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Irish EBF Ulster Oaks Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Thrifty Of Digby
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year, including over 1m at this venue; first two runs this term were moderate, including in a Sligo race in which Chico Guerrera was second; returning to form with a 7f win at Roscommon and backed that up over an extended 1m1f at Gowran with Chica Guerrera in third; can remain competitive despite an aggregate 10lb rise
runners.comment: In-form mare, can remain competitive despite an aggregate 10lb rise for her two wins

runners.name: Beauparc
runners.spotlight: In good form on AW during the winter and spring; stepped up from a moderate Sligo run when second in a 16-runner event over 1m4f at Gowran last time; generally reliable mare, though probably best over a little further than this.
runners.comment: Generally reliable mare, best suited by 1m4f, not far off her best at Gowran last time

runners.name: Diamond Exchange
runners.spotlight: Won AW maiden in January; returned to turf with good efforts in 1m2f handicaps at Leopardstown; soft ground may not have been ideal at the same venue on latest; helps to give Thrifty Of Digby's trainer a strong hand.
runners.comment: Soft ground may not have been ideal on latest, helps to give her trainer a strong hand
runners.owner: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin

runners.name: Stars Will Shine
runners.spotlight: Course 7f maiden winner last September; found wanting in a Group 3 race on heavy ground on final start; failed to make much impact over 1m at the Curragh on handicap/seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Course maiden winner at 7f, failed to make much impact over 1m on handicap/seasonal debut

runners.name: Chica Guerrera
runners.spotlight: Tramore 1m4f winner last August and finished the season well with placed efforts in big fields on heavy ground at the Curragh; has been showing consistent form; not sure to reverse recent form with Thrifty Of Digby.
runners.comment: Has been showing consistent form, not sure to reverse recent form with Thrifty Of Digby

runners.name: Apercu
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner; went very close on her only run over C&D in 2024; won twice in the early stages of last season; yet to hit form this year.
runners.comment: Five-time winner, went very close on her only run over C&D, yet to hit form this year

runners.name: Fiver Friday
runners.spotlight: Two wins in April, one of them over hurdles, the other a good display to beat the Irish Lincoln winner Ribee over 1m2f at the Curragh; solid fourth of 20 in a premier handicap at the same venue on latest; big weight here, but capable of holding her own.
runners.comment: Two wins in April, one of them over hurdles, solid fourth in a premier handicap on latest

runners.name: Narlita
runners.spotlight: Only two wins from 29 starts, but placed 12 times; met trouble in running at Sligo last time, two early-season Curragh runs were encouraging.
runners.comment: Met trouble in running at Sligo last time, two early-season Curragh runs were encouraging

runners.name: Liberation Date
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f maiden at Dundalk last December; 9lb out of the handicap; very tough task even with Orla Tynan's useful claim.
runners.comment: AW winner, 9lb out of the handicap, very tough task even with Orla Tynan's useful claim

runners.name: Chablis Rock
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on the second of two starts at two and began the season with a satisfactory display at the Curragh; had no trouble landing the odds in a Limerick maiden; holds an entry in the Irish Oaks and can be rated a strong contender for a yard in top form.
runners.comment: Had no trouble landing the odds in a Limerick maiden, definite chance for yard in top form

runners.name: Enchanted Lady
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced maiden; opening mark seems fair but several of her more experienced rivals are more appealing.
runners.comment: Lightly-raced maiden, opening mark seems fair but experienced rivals are more appealing

runners.name: Lady Lilac
runners.spotlight: Has done most of her racing at 1m, odds-on for her sole win in a Killarney maiden last autumn; this trip may stretch her stamina; on the plus side, this represents a drop in class after two Listed attempts.
runners.comment: Has done most of her racing at 1m, trip a worry, drops in class after two Listed attempts

runners.name: Huff'n'puff
runners.spotlight: Went up 2lb for a front-running Roscommon win; this longer trip should be within his range; has a bit to find with Mergus Serrator on Navan running.
runners.comment: Went up 2lb for a front-running Roscommon win, this longer trip should be within his range

runners.name: New Zealand
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a Curragh maiden last July; limitations exposed in stakes races, twice last year and in Listed events at Epsom and Navan this term; cheekpieces tried now; stable has won two of the last five editions of this race; unwise to rule out.
runners.comment: Limitations exposed in stakes races but of obvious interest as the stable's sole runner
