runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier/Michael Tabor/Derrick Smith/Peter M Brant

runners.name: Trojan Fighter
runners.spotlight: Unraced at two; showed plenty of improvement to land Ballinrobe maiden over this trip on his third start; met trouble in running in Navan race won by the smart Limestone; fair chance of reversing form with New Zealand on these terms; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Wide-margin maiden winner, met trouble in running in Navan race won by the smart Limestone

runners.name: Madbadanddangerous
runners.spotlight: Had been consistent before an inexplicable poor run at Navan; needs to bounce back from that; others preferred in the circumstances.
runners.comment: Had been consistent before an inexplicable poor run at Navan, needs to bounce back

runners.name: Pierre Grosse
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on his only handicap start; has failed to improve in two more recent attempts in maidens; 2lb wrong, rider claims a useful 5lb.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on his only handicap start, 2lb wrong, rider claims a useful 5lb

runners.name: Harmani
runners.spotlight: Twice beaten favourite in maidens following promising debut; more like it on handicap debut when good second in 1m Cork apprentice event; bolted up over the extended 1m2f here last month; may remain competitive off 14lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Bolted up over a shorter trip here last month, may remain competitive off 14lb higher

runners.name: Bay Of Stars
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner over 1m4f; ran on well over the same trip at Leopardstown in his first handicap; could go close if the addition of cheekpieces has a positive impact
runners.comment: AW maiden winner, ran on well over 1m4f in his first handicap, can make his presence felt

runners.name: Mergus Serrator
runners.spotlight: Respectable run at Leopardstown in October on only outing at two; shaped quite well when keeping on to take fourth in a 1m2f maiden at Navan in April, with subsequent winner Huff'N'Puff one place behind; appeared to find 1m inadequate at Killarney; this should suit better
runners.comment: Appeared to find 1m inadequate at Killarney, previous 1m2f run hinted at stamina reserves

runners.name: Shosholoza
runners.spotlight: Beat a subsequent winner by a wide margin on heavy ground at Tipperary on his debut last October; creditable fifth behind Pierre Bonnard in Group 1 at Saint-Cloud only three weeks later; soundly beaten in two Group 3s this season; hard to be sure if he can deliver on last year's promise.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in two Group 3s this season, yet to deliver on last year's promise
runners.owner: Rectory Road Hldgs Ltd/E Palmer/W Slattery/Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Wyman
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts in defeat in two starts over 1m and 7f last season; Leopardstown third over 1m2f was encouraging on return; steps up in trip again for his handicap debut; fair chance he will stay; others have more compelling claims
runners.comment: Steps up in trip for his handicap debut, fair chance he will stay, others preferred

runners.name: Grey Fable
runners.spotlight: Both turf wins came at Chester in 2024; surprise winner on AW at Dundalk last time given his poor form leading up to it, keeping on well over 1m4f; not certain to back that up off 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Surprise winner on AW last time given poor form leading to it; not certain to back it up

runners.name: Milverton
runners.spotlight: First reserve; yet to make an impact in Flat handicaps; well beaten at Fairyhouse on return.
runners.comment: Reserve; yet to make an impact in Flat handicaps; well beaten at Fairyhouse on return

runners.name: Deluca Chop
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner was wonderfully consistent in defeat on AW over winter/spring, placing in four consecutive runs at 1m4f-2m; continued that form back on turf at Roscommon (1m4f, yielding to soft) in May when again a runner-up; a visor replaced the previously worn cheekpieces at Navan (1m5f, good) last time and resulted in a victory; up 5lb but he's a player.
runners.comment: Consistency was rewarded with a win last time; up 5lb but should be on premises again

runners.name: Numidia
runners.spotlight: Sole win on turf came at Fairyhouse in June 2024 (1m4f, good); latest of three AW wins came at Dundalk in November over 2m; two turf runs this term were poor and most recent Dundalk outing was not his best either.
runners.comment: Sole turf win came in 2024 (three on AW); two turf runs this term poor

runners.name: Just Another Eagle
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; sole win came on AW in 2024 over 1m4f; went close off this mark at Navan in October; poor in two outings this season.
runners.comment: Reserve; AW winner went close off this mark at Navan in Oct'; poor twice this term

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Consistency was rewarded at Navan (1m5f, good) last time when he took advantage of being well placed throughout to score by 2.25l to gain a first win (1-19 on Flat); up 6lb, but should give his running again.
runners.comment: Consistency was rewarded at Navan when well placed to score; 1-19 on Flat now

runners.name: Plus Or Minus
runners.spotlight: No improvement for the switch to handicaps in his final two runs for Willie McCreery; tongue tie goes on for stable debut/seasonal return but hard to recommend on form.
runners.comment: No improvement for switch to h'caps last twice; tongue tied for stable debut

runners.name: Idomything
runners.spotlight: Won on soft ground at Bellewstown in October over an extended 1m4f off 1lb higher; moderate efforts since; chance at her best and rain will help.
runners.comment: Soft ground winner at Bellewstown in Oct'; moderate since but chance at best

runners.name: Jazz Forever
runners.spotlight: AW winner is 0-12 on turf; best effort for a long time came at Navan earlier this month when 0.5l behind Deluca Chop in third (1m5f, good); needs to repeat it off 2lb higher.
runners.comment: 0-12 on turf but best run for some time came last time when 0.5l behind Deluca Chop

runners.name: Folly Beach
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; 1-43 under rules with the win coming in 2023; midfield finish on return at Navan.
runners.comment: Reserve; 1-43 under rules with the win coming in 2023; midfield finish on return at Navan

runners.name: Takarengo
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner but his last win came in 2021; tailed off at Navan when last seen in this sphere and his runs over hurdles since don't provide much encouragement.
runners.comment: Three-time winner but last win came in 2021; no encouragement in recent runs

runners.name: Patrick Street
runners.spotlight: Bagged his second win on turf at Limerick in April (1m4f, yielding) off 44; failed to add an elusive win over obstacles in two starts since, although he placed over hurdles at Punchestown; could go well and has won off 1lb higher before.
runners.comment: Bagged second win on turf at Limerick in April; two runs over obstacles since; can go well

runners.name: Pebble Island
runners.spotlight: Snapped a dismal run of form when dropping into this grade at Fairyhouse and taking the runner-up spot (1m4f, good); nudged up 1lb but if he can build on that latest run he's a player.
runners.comment: Snapped a dismal run of form when runner-up last time; player if building on it

runners.name: Memories Maker
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran well enough in five Flat starts for Charlie Johnston in 2024 but his runs over hurdles were poor since then and he carried that poor form back to the Flat at Roscommon when last seen in July last year.
runners.comment: Ran okay in 2024 but poor over hurdles since and no improvement on Flat last time

runners.name: Yup Bro
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in all seven starts under rules including two AW handicaps; tailed off over hurdles last time; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Well beaten in seven starts under rules inc' two handicaps; hard to fancy

runners.name: Calmafterthestorm
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in this race in 2024 (good) off 3lb higher; lightly raced since but when she has run, she has been disappointing.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2024 off 3lb higher; disappointing in runs since

runners.name: Royal Alliance
runners.spotlight: AW winner for George Boughey in 2024; no positive Flat form since joining this yard; enters 0-60 company for the first time now.
runners.comment: AW winner in 2024; no positive Flat form since joining this yard; enters lower grade

runners.name: Steel Cut
runners.spotlight: Never counted in a 1m2f maiden at the Curragh on only start last year; shaped well when second to an 83-rated stabeknate of Duvessa at Dundalk after 12 months off; cheekpeiced added now
runners.comment: Never counted on only start last year, shaped well at Dundalk after 12 months off

runners.name: Duvessa
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well over 1m2f at the Curragh on sole start last year for Jessica Harrington; still looked raw when second to the Harrington-trained Marazion at Roscommon last month on her first start for Joseph O'Brien; should improve enough to play a leading role.
runners.comment: Ran once at three, still looked raw when second at Roscommon last month, should improve

runners.name: Joe Cool
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Ballinrobe, beating a Gordon Elliott-trained runner who was a Grade 3 AQPS winner last year; form of his second placing in a Bellewstown Flat maiden was shown in a good light by the winner Tim Toe when third in Tuesday's Ascot Stakes; missed an engagement at Leopardstown on Thursday because of changed ground; becomes a strong challenger here
runners.comment: Missed an engageement at Leopardstown on Thursday, capable of winning a race of this type

runners.name: Medadthebookie
runners.spotlight: Low-rated hurdler; has a Flat pedigree, but nothing to suggest he can feature here.
runners.comment: Low-rated hurdler, has a Flat pedigree but nothing to suggest he can feature here

runners.name: Annacroft
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister by Berkshire to useful hurdle/chase winner Noble Birth; dam placed 6f turf/AW, half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 1 winner Primo Valentino and 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Dorothea Carrington.
runners.comment: Half-sister to useful hurdle/chase winner Noble Birth; dam a well-related maiden

runners.name: Tears I Nevercried
runners.spotlight: Finished in mid-division at Roscommon on her second outing; well held by Duvessa on that showing.
runners.comment: Finished in mid-division at Roscommon, well held by Duvessa on that showing

runners.name: Counting Coup
runners.spotlight: Made the running in a Sligo bumper, dropped away in last half-mile; bred to stay over jumps; up against it here.
runners.comment: Made the running in a Sligo bumper, weakened in last half-mile, bred to stay over jumps

runners.name: Double Indian
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to six winners at trips ranging from 7f-1m4f; tailed off in a Kilbeggan bumper in April; can be left out of calculations, though he may be seen to better effect over this trip.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a Kilbeggan bumper in April, can be left out of calculations

runners.name: Murat
runners.spotlight: Won bumpers at Killarney and Galway, almost a year apart, making all on latter occasion; useful novice hurdler last season; took advantage of a mishap to El Cairos to win a maiden at Leopardstown at Christmas; remote third in a Grade 2 event at Fairyhouse at Eastweer; worth a shot in this sphere; possibly Galway-bound in due course
runners.comment: Unbeaten in two bumpers, useful novice hurdler last season, worth a shot in this sphere

runners.name: Daddy Long Legs
runners.spotlight: Grade 3 winner over hurdles, currently rated 150 after recent Ballinrobe win; previously may have needed the run when well beaten in a Southwell maiden won by Constitution Hill; went lame on only Irish Flat start, when favourite for a Ballinrobe maiden won by the 2025 Queen's Vase winner Carmers; Flat pedigree and went close over 1m4f at France in ; strong chance.
runners.comment: High-class hurdler, winner on latest, went lame on only Irish Flat start, strong chance

runners.name: Sea Celia's Star
runners.spotlight: Out of his depth in a Group 2 race in Germany last month; light on pedigree; no appeal.
runners.comment: Out of his depth in a Group 2 race in Germany last month, light on pedigree, no appeal

races.race_name: Tapestry Events EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Statten Island
runners.spotlight: Promising start to his career when fifth past the post (promoted to fourth) in a 6f novice at York last month; open to improvement and he looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Promising fourth at York (6f, good) on debut; more to come and he looks the one to beat

runners.name: Savoy Blue
runners.spotlight: 11,000euros yearling; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Rating (1m turf/9.4f AW; RPR 77), For A Laugh (US 7.5f-8.5f turf including 2yo) and King Savoy (Italian 7.5f/1m 2yo); dam 6f/7f 2yo winner (87), sister to 7f Listed winner Pausanias; stable not renowned for winning 2yo newcomers.
runners.comment: 11,000euros half-sister to three winners; stable not known for winning 2yo newcomers

runners.name: Just For Monty
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; closely related to 6f-7f winner Shark Two One (including 2yo; RPR 97), half-brother to five winners including Brian Ryan (5f-7f including AW/Group 3; 102), Kindly (6f; 92) and Prince Of Time (7f AW; 70); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (92); should have a future but this debut run may not be the time to catch him.
runners.comment: Six winning siblings and bred to have a future; should come on for this initial experience

runners.name: Maximus Meridius
runners.spotlight: 2,000gns breeze-up 2yo; first foal of a maiden sister to a winner in Italy; both the yard's 2yo winners this season had the benefit of a run but David Loughnane won this with an Amo Racing-owned newcomer in 2025.
runners.comment: 2,000gns breeze-up 2yo; stable won this with a newcomer in these colours in 2025
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: Thebesthasyetocome
runners.spotlight: Prominent in the betting ahead of his debut at Thirsk (5f, good) in April, a race where he was outpaced by the first two home; with those two horses having since won the Listed National Stakes and the Woodcote at Epsom, the form looks strong; 6f should suit and he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Third on Thirsk debut in April and the first and second have both won notable races since

runners.name: Beauty For Love
runners.spotlight: 20-1 when last of seven in a novice at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) seven weeks ago; the longer trip will suit and he should leave that initial effort well behind in time.
runners.comment: Last of seven at Newmarket (5f, good to firm) seven weeks ago; should do better in time

runners.name: Keep The Gold
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but placed three times, including over C&D; showed promise on soft ground on debut; blinkers replace cheekpieces; contender.
runners.comment: 0-8 but conditions should suit and he's slipping down the weights; switch of headgear

runners.name: King Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Ran out the easy winner of a handicap at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last month; up 6lb but slower ground won't faze him and he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Arrives on the back of an easy win at Beverley last month; one to consider

runners.name: Regal Dream
runners.spotlight: Promise as a 2yo but she suffered two heavy defeats in handicaps in April (sent off favourite for the first of them); new blinkers need to turn things around.
runners.comment: Promise as a 2yo; well beaten in two 5f handicaps in April; now tried in blinkers

runners.name: Wee Mary
runners.spotlight: She had been threatening on turf prior to a comfortable front-running success at Musselburgh (5f, good) two weeks ago; up 6lb; handles good to soft, unraced on slower.
runners.comment: Made all for comfortable success at Musselburgh two weeks ago; more needed up 6lb

runners.name: Ski Angel
runners.spotlight: All her wins have come on good or slower and with that in mind her recent second at Wetherby on good to firm was most encouraging; the more rain to fall the better and she's a major player.
runners.comment: Good second on Monday and today's slower ground very much in her favour; big player

runners.name: Henery Hawk
runners.spotlight: Seven-time AW winner but 0-21 on turf; ran well for third over C&D last month (good to firm) but the prospect of rain tempers enthusiasm.
runners.comment: Seven-time AW winner but 0-21 on turf; placed here latest but rain would be a worry

runners.name: Auntie Jo
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins for David O'Meara at Newcastle this year, the latest a smooth success in a 0-55 handicap 18 days ago; makes debut for her new stable off a 6lb higher mark with blinkers replacing other forms of headgear; below par in one run on ground slower than good.
runners.comment: Two 5f AW wins for D O'Meara this year; headgear switch on stable debut; rain is a concern
runners.owner: Making Headway Racing And K Scott

runners.name: Great Profit
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three runs for Richard Fahey last year; tailed off when 200-1 for his stable debut at Carlisle (7f, soft) last month; not easily recommended now handicapping back at 5f.
runners.comment: Little solid form; drops back to 5f for handicap debut; not easily recommended; new yard

runners.name: Royal Duke
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; plugged on for third behind Wee Mary at Musselburgh (5f, good) two weeks ago; on a dangerous mark but his yard may have stronger claims with Ski Angel.
runners.comment: C&D winner; third to Wee Mary at Musselburgh two weeks ago; stable also runs Ski Angel

runners.name: Supremissy
runners.spotlight: Best run so far when third of four behind the red-hot favourite at Hamilton (5f, good) seven weeks ago; faces a more realistic assignment now handicapping; betting to guide.
runners.comment: Offered more at Hamilton last time, albeit in potentially misleading race; betting useful

runners.name: Bonito Cavalo
runners.spotlight: Won six times on AW in the first part of 2024 but there hasn't been much to cheer about since; 0-27 on turf but has run well over C&D and the forecast rain won't bother him; still too much to prove for comfort.
runners.comment: Struggled since his purple patch in early 2024; conditions fine but too much to prove

races.race_name: Unite Natalie Egan Celebration Handicap (For The Johnstone Rose Bowl) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Square Necker
runners.spotlight: Won 1m4f AW maiden in December on second start then finished second to Constitution Hill at Southwell (1m4f, AW), before winning on handicap debut at Hamilton (1m5f, good) last month; showed promise on good to soft on debut; this unexposed 4yo could have more left in the tank; major player.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo who won on handicap debut at Hamilton and could have more left in the tank

runners.name: Divine Knight
runners.spotlight: 5yo who won for Sean Woods on belated debut last July; mixed results across his five starts since and he's unraced beyond 1m2f, but he stumbled leaving the stalls when mid-division on last month's stable and seasonal debut at Redcar, and things could click at some point; not written off.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo; stumbled leaving stalls on yard debut; things could click at some point

runners.name: Dancingwithmyself
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft) in April on reappearance and bounced back from lesser performance when third at Goodwood (1m6f, good) last month; can be slowly away but every chance she'll be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Some good form in defeat this year and every chance she'll be thereabouts

runners.name: Letsbefrank
runners.spotlight: He's reappeared in rude health this year with wins under Lauren Young at Musselburgh (1m4f, good) and Hamilton (1m3f, good) before good third at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last time; he'll be fine at this longer trip, and also if slow ground prevails; could be bang there.
runners.comment: He's reappeared in rude health this year and could be bang there

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Slow starter whose losing run goes back 16 races to March 2025; however, he's been second on three of his last four starts, most recently at York (1m4f, good) last Saturday; slow ground is probably fine; each-way chance.
runners.comment: Losing run continues but runner-up on three of his last four starts; each-way chance

runners.name: Peaky Blinder
runners.spotlight: Won at Redcar (1m6f, good to soft) last October on second handicap start and followed up on reappearance at Southwell (2m, AW) in April; tailed off in the Chester Cup (2m2f, good) since but showed signs of post-race ataxia; retains potential and could bounce back with a bold show.
runners.comment: Won two in a row prior to Chester Cup flop but showed signs of post-race ataxia; unexposed

runners.name: Pole Star
runners.spotlight: Good third in first-time visor (retained since) at Newmarket (1m6f, good to firm) last month and again ran well at Thirsk (2m, good) last time, when outstayed; last win came on soft ground last July; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Good third over 1m6f at Newmarket then outstayed over 2m at Thirsk; last win came on soft
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Alba Chiara
runners.spotlight: She's struggled on both starts this season and a major turnaround is needed; she was progressive during last season's 3yo campaign and has travelled from Northern Ireland to race today; one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: She's struggled this term but has travelled from Northern Ireland; one to watch in betting
runners.owner: Mrs Tracey O'Hare & D W Sloan & W Jones

runners.name: Ashnak
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who was third at Ripon (1m4f, good) in April on second stable start before again showing promise when fourth of 15 at York (10.2f, good); won on heavy ground in France; not ruled out but there is a question mark regarding the suitability of this step up in trip.
runners.comment: In good heart for new yard; not ruled out but question mark about stamina at this trip

runners.name: Boatswain
runners.spotlight: Contested some tough turf handicaps in last season's 3yo campaign and, having dropped down the weights, he returned to form when third at Kempton (1m4f, AW) last month on his second start of the year; kept on well last time and today's extra yardage may well be a positive; chance.
runners.comment: Kept on for third over 1m4f at Kempton and today's extra yardage may well be a positive
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Albany
runners.spotlight: 6yo who ran just three times last year, twice soundly beaten but going close at York (1m4f, good) on the middle outing; her wins have come on heavy/good to soft ground; capable of playing a leading role on return.
runners.comment: Just three outings last year, going close on middle run; capable of playing leading role

runners.name: Oneforthegutter
runners.spotlight: His form went the wrong way last season and he was soundly beaten on last month's return at Newmarket; however, he may have needed that run and is 2lb lower than when winning at Newmarket (1m6f, good to firm) last July; versatile ground-wise; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but 2lb below last winning mark and not ruled out

runners.name: Lope Y Linda
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a 7f handicap at Catterick (good) last month; second over 1m a fortnight ago but she took a backwards step at Beverley on Tuesday; needs to bounce back quickly but this is weak.
runners.comment: Below par on Tuesday but running well in defeat beforehand; not discounted

runners.name: Trais Fluors
runners.spotlight: Veteran who gained the latest of his 13 wins at Newcastle on New Year's Day; mixed bag since but he now drops into a classified race and should make a bold bid; rain won't bother him.
runners.comment: 13-time winner; drops into a 0-50 and the veteran should give it a good go

runners.name: Lanarra
runners.spotlight: Progressive in low-level 6f handicaps, winning at Newcastle in October and placed in both runs this summer; step back up to 7f can help and she won't mind if the rain arrives.
runners.comment: Progressive in 6f handicaps; return to 7f may help and won't mind if it rains; good chance

runners.name: Monhammer
runners.spotlight: Two of his four wins have come over C&D; tenth of 11 behind Approaching Dawn at Musselburgh (1m, good) two weeks ago on his first run for seven months; best watched.
runners.comment: Low-key reappearance at Musselburgh two weeks ago; may need another run or two

runners.name: Ridgemaster
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 66-1 surprise (beat No Nay Nevermind) in a 7f handicap at Newcastle on his stable debut in December; three poor runs followed but he returned to form when third at Ripon (6f, good) last month; chance back up in distance.
runners.comment: 7f AW win in December; back to form when 3rd at Ripon last month; contender back at 7f

runners.name: Pebble Dash
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; attracted support at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) ten days ago but he was passively ridden before finishing with a flourish to take fourth; going back over 7f can help and he holds each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Set plenty to do but finished well for 4th latest (6f); extra furlong a plus; e-w claims

runners.name: No Nay Nevermind
runners.spotlight: Exposed 20-race maiden; this winter's AW form brings her into the reckoning but she has been well beaten in two runs back on turf.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden; chance on this winter's best but well held on turf on last two starts
runners.owner: S Jones, L Donaldson & K Elliott

runners.name: Queenies Pal
runners.spotlight: Little solid form and she was a well-beaten 50-1 shot at Redcar on last month's seasonal return; tongue-tie on for the first time and cheekpieces get a second airing.
runners.comment: Little solid form and she was well beaten at Redcar on her seasonal return; tongue tied

runners.name: Ring Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Five AW wins on his record but 0-23 on turf; well beaten on turf on his last two starts (around 1m) and dropping in trip isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: 0-23 on turf, including two poor runs last month; others look safer

runners.name: Royal Baroness
runners.spotlight: She didn't run badly on her reappearance at Carlisle (1m, soft) last month but she was a remote third of six at Musselburgh two weeks ago; stable also runs Lanarra.
runners.comment: Didn't build on her promising reappearance last time; stable also runs Lanarra

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 6yo who found a jolt of improvement when winning a 1m handicap at Musselburgh (good ground) two weeks ago; however, he lost all chance when blowing the start here (1m, soft; 4-1) last night and was last of nine throughout.
runners.comment: 1m win at Musselburgh two weeks ago was big improvement; blew the start here on Friday
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Evelyn's Garden
runners.spotlight: Not shown a huge amount in her five starts; only 6-1 for her turf debut at Carlisle (6f, soft) 12 days ago but finished eighth of nine; needs to raise her game.
runners.comment: Not shown much in her five starts; may do better but she's not a solid option

races.race_name: Unite George Ramsay Celebration EBF Land O'Burns Fillies' Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Beautiful Diamond
runners.spotlight: 3-3 at Ayr, all in Listed races including a dead-heat in this race in 2024; ran another fine race when third to classy stablemate Night Raider in the Group 2 Temple Stakes four weeks ago; obvious chance back down in grade and rain won't bother her.
runners.comment: 3-3 at Ayr, all in Listed company including this race in 2024; good 3rd latest; key player

runners.name: Kinswoman
runners.spotlight: Progressive sprinter in 2025; promising reappearance last month and she was sent off favourite for the Dash at Epsom on the back of it; seemed to find that sharp test all a bit much but she was keeping on at the finish and this more conventional test will suit; likely to make an impact at this level in time.
runners.comment: Only 6th in the Dash last time but this more conventional track will suit; still unexposed

runners.name: Rose Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Looked a good prospect when winning 6f novices at Kempton and Southwell last September; maintained her unbeaten record with a narrow success in a handicap at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) seven weeks ago; drops to 5f on the slowest ground she will have faced but hard to knock an unbeaten filly.
runners.comment: 3-3, all over 6f (AW/good to firm); still unexposed but faced with a different test today

runners.name: Coconut Cove
runners.spotlight: All runs over 6f; best form when fifth of 15 in a Haydock Listed event four weeks ago; drop to 5f needs to spark improvement but it isn't out of the question.
runners.comment: All runs over 6f; improvement required for drop to 5f but it is not out of the question

runners.name: Luna A Inbhir Nis
runners.spotlight: Had a wonderful year in 2025, winning six times and rounding off her campaign with a good fifth behind Beautiful Diamond in a Listed event over 5.5f here (soft); down the field on her return but she quickly rebounded with a fine third at Carlisle (Listed; 5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; still improving but the forecast rain may suit others better.
runners.comment: Speedy and progressive filly; personal best when 3rd in Carlisle Listed latest; contender

runners.name: Shes Got A Brother
runners.spotlight: Won two 5f events on AW in April and she has performed with great credit in two hot 5f handicaps this summer, third of 16 in the 3yo Dash two weeks ago; open to further progress but this may come too soon in her career; hooded for the first time.
runners.comment: Unexposed/progressive sprinter; this big rise in class may come too early in her career

runners.name: Argentine Tango
runners.spotlight: Listed winner as a 2yo (6f, good to firm); running creditably in defeat this year and has the second highest BHA rating in the field but likely vulnerable to anything progressive.
runners.comment: Listed winner at two; running creditably this year but vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Layla Liz
runners.spotlight: First low-key effort when last of ten in a Class 3 handicap at Nottingham (5f, good) in April; won't mind if it rains but she will need a clear personal best to trouble the principals.
runners.comment: Flopped in a Class 3 handicap at Nottingham when last seen; big personal best required

runners.name: Fluorescence
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and progressive filly; sat back as the stalls opened and unseated her rider soon after on her reappearance at Thirsk three weeks ago; she will need a career best at this level but could have more to come.
runners.comment: Progressive last year; had a mishap at the stalls on her return; still capable of better

runners.name: Cardiff By The Sea
runners.spotlight: Bumped into Charles Darwin on 2yo debut and then ran a cracker for fourth in the Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; made all to win a maiden at Cork (5f, soft) on her reappearance but below par in a Naas Listed race when last seen; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Chance on her best form but she failed to fire in a Naas Listed race when last seen
runners.owner: P J O'Brien, Mrs P Shanahan & M O'Flynn

runners.name: Sapphire Princess
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt when winning a 7f fillies' maiden at Kempton last October (RPR 78); stiff task starting out for new yard (same owner) over a new trip.
runners.comment: 7f AW maiden win when last seen in October; stiff task over new trip on stable debut

runners.name: Thora
runners.spotlight: 6.5f AW win in France when trained by Amy Murphy; fifth in a Listed race in March (cheekpieces first time), after which she moved to Kevin Ryan's stable; headgear off today and she needs improvement for the drop to 5f.
runners.comment: Something to find on stable debut; first run at 5f; cheekpieces absent today

runners.name: Rhyme Dust
runners.spotlight: Third to Beautiful Diamond over 5.5f here (soft) in September on her final run for Mark Usher; low-key start for Ralph Beckett but she bounced back to form at York (6f, good) eight days ago, blowing the start but making a big mid-race move before weakening into fourth close home; shaped like the best horse in that race and perhaps has a stronger chance than her rating might indicate.
runners.comment: Third to Beautiful Diamond here last September (5.5f, soft); very promising 4th last week

runners.name: Thenandnow
runners.spotlight: Beat 23 rivals when winning a maiden at the Curragh (6f, soft) on her seasonal return; well held in Listed event last month; big step forward required.
runners.comment: Won a big-field maiden at the Curragh in April but safely held in Listed company last time

runners.name: Billie Be Quick
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in seven races (six of them Listed) since winning a Class 4 handicap at Chepstow (5f, good to soft) last August; ran okay on Sunday but she will need more to take this; blinkers the headgear of choice.
runners.comment: Not beaten far at Cork on Sunday but she remains vulnerable at this level

runners.name: Circle Of Trust
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but ran well when second in Thirsk maiden (7f, good to soft; 9-2) two weeks ago; a repeat of that run would put him firmly in the mix.
runners.comment: Second in Thirsk maiden two weeks ago and a repeat of that would put him firmly in the mix
runners.owner: Mrs R G Hillen And Partner

runners.name: Ellie's de Vega
runners.spotlight: Regularly makes the frame, third over 5.5f at Wetherby on latest start; however, he looks fully exposed and a wide draw may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Regularly makes the frame but looks fully exposed and wide draw may not be ideal

runners.name: Milford Grange
runners.spotlight: Knocking at the door and beaten only by the other joint-favourite on handicap debut at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) five weeks ago, rallying; shapes as if this longer trip will be right up his street; major player.
runners.comment: Knocking at the door; second on handicap debut at Doncaster; major player

runners.name: Napolian
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but did finish in the first three on first three 2yo starts; gelded before eighth of 13 on handicap debut at Thirsk (6f, good; 12-1) two weeks ago; has stamina to prove over this longer trip but may come on for the run.
runners.comment: 0-5; stamina to prove over this longer trip but may come on for reappearance run

runners.name: Real Jack
runners.spotlight: Placed just once from six outings and his best efforts so far have come on the AW; below par at Thirsk two weeks ago; needs first-time cheekpieces to give him a lift.
runners.comment: Placed just once from six outings; needs first-time cheekpieces to give him a lift

runners.name: Storm Esme
runners.spotlight: 2-3 last year but has yet to show she has trained on, tailed off at Pontefract last time; moves up in trip with plenty to prove.
runners.comment: 2-3 last year but has yet to show she has trained on; plenty to prove

runners.name: Magistery
runners.spotlight: Won soft-ground 6f nursery at Carlisle last September but hasn't come close to repeating the form, folding tamely on reappearance; opposable.
runners.comment: Won nursery at Carlisle last September but hasn't come close to repeating the form

runners.name: Horace Wallace
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in 6f Wolverhampton novice in February but has failed to repeat the form and was well down the field on turf debut at Hamilton last month; needs today's longer trip to spark a revival.
runners.comment: Has failed to repeat form of sole win and was well down the field on turf debut last time

runners.name: Blue Mantle
runners.spotlight: Blasted off in front over 1m last time and didn't get home; went down narrowly over this trip at Newcastle in March; still low-mileage and the return to 7f should suit.
runners.comment: Went off too fast last time; still low-mileage and this return to 7f should suit

runners.name: Sanny Doo
runners.spotlight: Beaten at least 14l all four starts; may be a late-developer but difficult to recommend from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Beaten at least 14l all four starts; difficult to recommend from out of the weights

runners.name: Novamay
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a 6f hat-trick last July, one of them here, scaling the handicap in the process; promising run at Redcar (6f, good to firm) last month, her second run of the year, and she now has something to build on; unraced on slower than good but she is by Mayson out a mare whose best form came on slower than good; interesting.
runners.comment: C&D winner; promising run at Redcar latest; one to be interested in

runners.name: Roberto Caro
runners.spotlight: Drawn wide when down the field at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) last time; his earlier 6f form brings him right into the reckoning and rain would be a positive; solid chance.
runners.comment: Latest run forgivable and dropping back to 6f in his favour; rain no issue; solid chance

runners.name: Lion's House
runners.spotlight: A contender on his AW form this year but latest fourth to Parisiac at Hamilton (5f, good) doesn't suggest he is the answer this time.
runners.comment: A contender on this year's AW form but he has a bit more to prove on turf

runners.name: Kelpie Grey
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, all over 7f-1m, but none since May 2024; has run well over C&D in the past and rain won't bother him; beaten favourite in Class 5 events on his last two starts and this is tougher.
runners.comment: Let his supporters down in Class 5 events on last two starts; risky up in class

runners.name: Falcon Queen
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish filly; 33-1 and well beaten on last month's stable debut at Hamilton (6f, good to firm); hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Well-beaten 33-1 shot on last month's stable debut; hard to fancy

runners.name: Whiskey Kisses
runners.spotlight: Consistent rather than progressive for George Scott; sold for 6,500gns in April; stiff task on stable debut.
runners.comment: Didn't progress for George Scott; sold 6,500gns in April; stiff task on stable debut

runners.name: Dandy Magic
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good run of form between September and February, winning twice (including here; 5f, good to soft); absent since a disappointing effort 116 days ago; one of five runners for Jim Goldie.
runners.comment: Not handicapped out of things but absent for 116 days; stable run five here

runners.name: Parisiac
runners.spotlight: He has returned from a break in top form, winning a 5f handicap at Hamilton (good) before a solid second at Carlisle (5f, good to soft) 11 days ago; returning to 6f no issue and he will be fine if it rains; should go well.
runners.comment: Two fine runs over 5f this year; no problem with either 6f or rainfall; one to consider

runners.name: Aberama Gold
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner; successful at Catterick (6f, good) in April but held in more competitive races since; not handicapped out of things but might prove vulnerable for win purposes.
runners.comment: 3-time C&D winner; won at Catterick in April but comfortably held in deeper races since

runners.name: Oriental Prince
runners.spotlight: Seven wins in 2025, including a turf success (7f, good to firm) off a 3lb higher mark; this looks more manageable than the 20-runner 0-85 handicap he contested at York eight days ago; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Not at his best in two turf runs this summer but we know he is capable of better

runners.name: Abduction
runners.spotlight: Two wins this year, the latest off this mark at Musselburgh (7f, good) in April; penultimate Hamilton seventh suggested he was still in good form; no surprise to see him in the thick of it.
runners.comment: Talented 8yo; on a winning mark and could leave his recent efforts well behind

runners.name: Prince Of Pillo
runners.spotlight: Two course wins as a 2yo, including a Listed success over 5f; last year's two wins came on AW but he is down in the weights ahead of this return to action and his course record means he can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: 2-2 at Ayr; well handicapped on 2025 AW form; not ruled out back from a break

runners.name: Eternal Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Got on a roll in the second half of 2025, completing a quick hat-trick when landing the Portland Handicap at Doncaster (5.6f, good) in September; yet to get going this year and drop in class isn't enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Had a good time in 2025 but out of sorts this year; down in class but risky

runners.name: Tinto
runners.spotlight: Operating off a reduced mark but on a losing run; has strong C&D form but reserves his best for Thirsk these days; well beaten on last month's seasonal return.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark but didn't do enough on his reappearance to earn the vote

runners.name: Timely Affair
runners.spotlight: Very competitive since he got a mark, with form figures of 2152213; return to turf proved no problem when fairly close third of ten at Yarmouth (1m, good) one month ago and he's threatening to take after his only sibling, who has proved relentlessly progressive for these connections; big player.
runners.comment: Form figures of 2152213 since he got a mark; further progress likely; big player

runners.name: King Of Charm
runners.spotlight: Endured a fair spell in the doldrums until cheekpieces woke him up when chasing home Siam Fox at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) three weeks ago; very well handicapped if that signals a revival in his fortunes.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces woke him up when chasing home Siam Fox at Yarmouth three weeks ago; chance

runners.name: Gladiadora
runners.spotlight: Made a winning reappearance at Southwell (7f, AW) in March and ran well on turf and AW on her next two starts, all under Liam Wright; blew out as favourite at Yarmouth last time, when her trainer blamed rain-softened ground; 1m fast-ground novice winner who could well bounce back; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Has gelled with Liam Wright; excuse latest; tongue-tie added; could bounce back
runners.owner: Mrs R Coleman And G Glendining

runners.name: Valentine Boy
runners.spotlight: Majority of starts on AW and he scored on that surface at Kempton (1m) off 4lb lower in May; things didn't pan out ideally when good fourth of 14 over that C&D three weeks later and he brings some momentum to this switch back to turf.
runners.comment: Running well on AW and he brings some momentum to this switch back to turf; respected

runners.name: Siam Fox
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner who finished a close third in this race off 9lb higher last year; took advantage of a slipping mark when making all to beat King Of Charm at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) in this headgear combination three weeks ago and a 4lb nudge still leaves him competitively weighted.
runners.comment: Took advantage of slipping mark to beat King Of Charm in this headgear last time

runners.name: Pay Attention
runners.spotlight: Completely missed the break but showed best form since last July when second at Newbury (1m, good) in this first-time headgear combination under Oliver Carmichael one month ago; 1lb nudge still leaves him below his winning mark and he needs considering.
runners.comment: Best form since last July when second under Oliver Carmichael one month ago; considered

runners.name: Freddie's Star
runners.spotlight: 1-16 shot when he won a weak novice at Chelmsford in November (7f, AW) but he hasn't kicked on in three handicaps subsequently and it was a heavy defeat at Newbury 19 days ago; drop in grade with first-time cheekpieces the straws to clutch at.
runners.comment: Heavy defeat latest; drop in grade with first-time cheekpieces the straws to clutch at

runners.name: Atlantic Sunset
runners.spotlight: Visored for the first time when scoring here (1m4f, good to firm) last June for his previous yard, but he hasn't beaten a rival in two starts for this stable; takes a marked drop in trip from plummeting mark in added cheekpieces so worth a cursory market glance; would be a first winner on her third ride for Megan Smith.
runners.comment: Last both starts for yard; marked drop in trip from plummeting mark in added cheekpieces

runners.name: Born A Rebel
runners.spotlight: Three turf wins include a 20-1 7f success when tongue tied here (good) last July; posted a cracking return from a break when 33-1 and second of 16 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) in April but it all went pear-shaped at Chester last Friday; has won at 1m before but last year's efforts over this trip weren't overly convincing.
runners.comment: 7f win here; all went pear-shaped latest and last year's efforts at 1m weren't convincing

runners.name: Didaar
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden who was far from disgraced in a brief spell with Denis Quinn, including when beaten a fraction over 3l despite not getting much luck at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) last time; career-low mark and this return to 1m and turf is no problem on his stable debut.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in a brief spell with Denis Quinn; career-low mark and 1m/turf no problem

runners.name: Into Combat
runners.spotlight: Modest form in his qualifying runs and two starts in handicaps didn't spark improvement last year; well bred, however, and it might be significant connections are keeping faith; needs a market check ahead of this reappearance.
runners.comment: Modest form but well bred and it may be significant connections keep faith; market check

races.race_name: JCB British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Al Hudaiba
runners.spotlight: Came away with a subsequent winner (Mrair, who wasn't beaten far in the Coventry at Ascot in the week) on his 6f debut on the Rowley Mile last month; looked set to follow up under a penalty until unseating at Yarmouth next time; the one to beat.
runners.comment: Looked set to follow up under penalty until unseating at Yarmouth latest; the one to beat

runners.name: Down To You Kid
runners.spotlight: £82,000 yearling who made a solid 6f debut last month; out of a 7f winner (half-sister to a 7f 2yo winner), so the extra furlong should be fine, but that effort has been let down by the principals since (second and fourth beaten at short prices next time).
runners.comment: Solid 6f debut last month; that effort has been let down by the principals since though

runners.name: Under Arrest
runners.spotlight: Early-foaled brother to the yard's ordinary maiden Keep It Classic; Harry Burns boasts a decent strike-rate for his trainer (22%), who is still to get rolling with 2yos for the year (0-2 so far) and has a surprisingly poor record with juveniles round here.
runners.comment: Brother to yard's maiden Keep It Classic; just his trainer's third 2yo runner this season

runners.name: Love Rome
runners.spotlight: 120,000euros yearling who's a half-brother to four winners, notably Ontario (placed in a Group 2) and Ivy League (placed in a Group 3), both for Aidan O'Brien; the market will shed some light.
runners.comment: 120,000euros yearling; half-brother to four winners; the market will shed some light

runners.name: Kaito
runners.spotlight: Prominent in the market for his 7f Sandown debut last weekend but badly needed the experience, fluffing the start and running green before finishing a remote last of seven; entitled to improve but will need a big step forward.
runners.comment: Badly needed the experience last weekend; entitled to improve but needs a big step forward
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: JCB Fastrac EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Alfaraz
runners.spotlight: Did well to finish as close in a warm novice on comeback given he pulled hard to the start and in the race; again inclined to over-race in a hood (now removed) when bombing at odds-on last time and a lot will hinge on how he behaves dropping from 1m2f.
runners.comment: Again inclined to over-race last time; lot will hinge on how he behaves dropping from 1m2f

runners.name: Cockade
runners.spotlight: Last winter's debut fourth well advertised since and he did his bit when winning easily on his Windsor comeback 12 days ago; this is tougher under a penalty, for a yard that won this with Ombudsman two years ago, but he's open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Won easily on Windsor comeback 12 days ago; open to further improvement under a penalty

runners.name: Moreedd
runners.spotlight: Well-related colt who was well held at odds-on on his debut in the spring; much better upped to this trip last time (good to soft), winning despite hanging left, but will need another decent step forward under the penalty.
runners.comment: Better upped to this trip last time, despite hanging left; needs another good step forward

runners.name: Littleferry
runners.spotlight: Daughter of the yard's fair handicapper Hope Is High (1m2f-1m6f); likely she'll want more time and is probably one for handicaps over further down the line.
runners.comment: Likely she'll want more time and is probably one for handicaps over further down the line

runners.name: Zynak
runners.spotlight: Big prices and well beaten in two runs over 1m2f thus far; likely his future lies in handicaps and he can't be recommended today.
runners.comment: Likely his future lies in handicaps and he can't be recommended today

runners.name: Sea Cookie
runners.spotlight: Improved a touch on last summer's runner-up efforts, back from gelding and wind operations, when winning an ordinary-looking Newbury maiden early in the month; lightly raced still but will find this tougher now handicapping.
runners.comment: Entitled to improve and lightly raced still but will find this tougher now handicapping

runners.name: Sapphire Steps
runners.spotlight: Highly tried after a successful debut last year; never involved upped to 1m last time, having made a solid comeback over 7f, and the drop to sprinting would suggest stamina may have been the issue; one to consider down another 2lb.
runners.comment: Highly tried after a successful debut last year; one to consider down 2lb dropped from 1m
runners.owner: J Palmer-Brown, C Engel And Partner

runners.name: Dutch Finale
runners.spotlight: Had a couple of wide stalls to deal with over 7f on the AW lately and may yet have more to offer as a sprinter; interesting to see how he goes in the market, back from nine months off, for a yard also responsible for Expert Agent.
runners.comment: May yet have more to offer as a sprinter; interesting to see how he goes in the market

runners.name: Expert Agent
runners.spotlight: C&D winner this time last year who bounced back to form off 6lb lower on the Rowley Mile five weeks ago; not been this high in the weights for a good while but having Jack Callan (second ride for the yard) take off 3lb will help and he's high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Not been this high in the weights for a good while but still goes on the shortlist
runners.owner: J P Morris, Mrs V A Tunnicliffe Et Al

runners.name: Bintola
runners.spotlight: 625,000gns yearling who offered bits of promise in three runs in a hood last autumn; very easy to back ahead of her handicap debut four weeks ago but won well and remains open to improvement upped 7lb.
runners.comment: Easy to back ahead of her handicap debut but won well and open to improvement upped 7lb

runners.name: Romantic Twilight
runners.spotlight: Best efforts last year came from the front but she had no chance of leading on her comeback behind Bintola four weeks ago thanks to a slow start; should do better then, but she was a big price at Windsor and is fully exposed now.
runners.comment: Well behind behind Bintola a month ago; could do better if leading but fully exposed now

runners.name: Twilight Fun
runners.spotlight: Only turf win came off a much lower mark more than two years ago and he remains weighted to his AW best after a couple of no-shows at Yarmouth last month; some way down the pecking order.
runners.comment: Remains weighted to his AW best after a couple of no-shows at Yarmouth last month

runners.name: Goldwork
runners.spotlight: Didn't look to see it out on his final two runs last year and 7f has again looked beyond him the past twice, off the back of a solid AW comeback off 2lb higher; today's stiff 6f should be more up his street and he's worth a second look.
runners.comment: 7f has again looked beyond him the past twice; well worth a second look

runners.name: Valley Ofthe Kings
runners.spotlight: Drawn away from the action on his return to turf but was then said to have been unsuited by the undulations of Catterick last time, which would be a concern round here; 2lb above his last winning mark and has a bit to prove back from a short break.
runners.comment: 2lb above his last winning mark and has a bit to prove back from a short break

runners.name: Dojin
runners.spotlight: A rising force who is 3-5 on AW overall and won 1m3f/1m4f handicaps at Kempton on her two starts this season; both were by a neck and, despite her pedigree, she now looks worth a go at 1m6f after the good scrap last time; up 5lb but turf debut may ask the bigger question.
runners.comment: Rising force who looks worth a go at 1m6f; up 5lb but this is also her turf debut

runners.name: Sixpack
runners.spotlight: In the groove this season and hit career-best form when winning a five-runner race from off the pace at Catterick (1m6f) nine days ago; up 3lb but highly respected.
runners.comment: In the groove this season and hit career-best form for Catterick win (1m6f) nine days ago

runners.name: Kuredu King
runners.spotlight: 11.6f and 1m6f handicap winner last year for the Gosdens; soundly beaten on both attempts over hurdles for new yard but fourth of nine at 33-1 back on the Flat at Chester (2m) three weeks ago was much more like it; needs to build on that.
runners.comment: Fourth of nine at 33-1 back on the Flat at Chester (2m) was much more like it

runners.name: Roadlesstravelled
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 novice hurdle winner in November 2024 before twice pulled up in that sphere; absent 356 days before three AW runs over 1m3f/1m4f this February/March to start his career under Flat rules, twice behind Constitution Hill no less and he did show significant ability; 1m6f should suit on this handicap debut; one to note.
runners.comment: 1m6f should suit on this Flat handicap debut; one to note, including on hurdles form

runners.name: Youthful King
runners.spotlight: Usually apprentice ridden but Jude Fernandes rides him for the first time; 6lb lower than when winning at Sandown (1m6f, good to firm) last June; he has not beaten many in his four starts this season, coming last in a four-runner race last time, but can't be dismissed and the hood he often wore last term (and first run this year) is reinstated.
runners.comment: Won last June (1m6f, good to firm) off 6lb higher; he's not beaten many this term

runners.name: Ventana
runners.spotlight: Has had only three goes, and the drop from 7f to 6f seemed to suit at Windsor (good to firm) last time with an improved second of ten; moves into handicaps with untapped potential.
runners.comment: Improved second at Windsor last time and moves into handicaps with untapped potential

runners.name: Zoustar Dreams
runners.spotlight: Revived for the fitting of cheekpieces recently, making all at Catterick (6f, good to firm) and Lingfield (5f, soft); edging back up the weights but in the form of her life and should go well again.
runners.comment: Revived for the fitting of cheekpieces recently, making all twice; should go well again

runners.name: Startled Lady
runners.spotlight: 0-9 and her form has plateaued; no obvious excuse when fifth of eight at Lingfield last time; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 0-9 and form has plateaued; no obvious excuse when fifth at Lingfield last time

runners.name: Mortubo
runners.spotlight: Much improved for the fitting of a tongue-strap at Bath (5.7f, good) two weeks ago, leading 1f out and winning going away; a 7lb rise looks fair and she should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Much improved for fitting of tongue-tie when winning at Bath; should make another bold bid

runners.name: Reality Queen
runners.spotlight: Lost her way for Jonathan Portman after winning at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) last September; however, she has dropped down to the same mark and makes her debut for a yard that has done well with recruits from other stables; interesting, especially if the market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: Has dropped down to mark of her sole win and could be revived by change of stable

runners.name: Spacewoman
runners.spotlight: Promise in novice/maiden company but has not made the frame in four handicaps and cheekpieces failed to halt the slide last time.
runners.comment: Has not made the frame in four handicaps; cheekpieces failed to halt the slide last time

runners.name: Nifty
runners.spotlight: Won twice over this trip as a 2yo but has yet to show she's trained on, beating just one opponent in three starts this season; enough to prove.
runners.comment: Won twice over this trip as a 2yo but has yet to show she's trained on; enough to prove

runners.name: Artista
runners.spotlight: Gained two 5f wins (good to firm and AW) last summer but has found things tougher this year; drops in grade after contesting the 3yo Dash at Epsom last time but she carries risks nonetheless.
runners.comment: 0-6 this term; drops in grade after contesting the 3yo Dash at Epsom but still risky

runners.name: Bluebluelectricblu
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all five outings, although not beaten far at Wolverhampton on third run last term; needs to build on seventh of 12 at Haydock (7f) on return, and today's drop back in distance may help.
runners.comment: Unplaced all five outings; might have needed reappearance; this drop back in trip may help

runners.name: Diomed Duke
runners.spotlight: His wins have come from the front in the summer, including this time last year on his second run back; turned in a satisfactory comeback at Windsor, when losing a shoe, and is entitled to have come on for that first start in seven months; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Entitled to have come on for his recent first start in seven months; shortlisted

runners.name: Court Drive
runners.spotlight: Took advantage of a sliding mark on her return to turf when winning a soft-ground 0-65 off 3lb lower ten days ago; remains on a fair mark now back in form but this is tougher and she's having a first run over 5f in the best part of a year.
runners.comment: Back in form but this is tougher; having a first run over 5f in the best part of a year

runners.name: Tuscan Point
runners.spotlight: Back to his best since joining his current yard but he's shot up the weights and there have been some costly defeats to go with two AW wins; now 0-17 on turf, having pulled hard in a messy Bath affair last time, and will need a career-best effort to take this.
runners.comment: Back to his best since joining this yard; 0-17 on turf though, and will need a career best

runners.name: Shavkat
runners.spotlight: Far from exposed, having been picked up from Roger Varian for 20,000gns, but may not be the easiest and will need to get away better; hung left last time (only has daylight on that side from stall 7) and goes in first-time cheekpieces/tongue-tie.
runners.comment: May not be the easiest and needs to get away better; first-time cheekpieces/tongue-tie on

runners.name: Coyy
runners.spotlight: A slow start did for him in the Epsom Dash last time and this is less competitive over a stiffer 5f; current mark should be within range if he gets away better and finds the race setting up for him.
runners.comment: This is less competitive over a stiffer 5f; current mark should be within range; player

runners.name: Quantum Power
runners.spotlight: Lacked consistency since handicapping but having Jack Callan taking over won't hurt down 2lb and this mark should be within range; one to consider.
runners.comment: Having Jack Callan taking over won't hurt down 2lb and this mark should be within range

runners.name: Good Earth
runners.spotlight: Ideally suited by an easy 6f or stiff 5f and knows how to win; turned in a solid effort at his beloved Sandown last weekend and remains reliable despite his age, but there's not much room for manoeuvre off this mark.
runners.comment: Remains reliable despite his age, but there's not much room for manoeuvre off this mark

races.race_name: Barry O'Neill Memorial EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands C/D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Furturra
runners.spotlight: Has already had seven starts but he made the breakthrough at Musselburgh (5f, good) two weeks ago and was a good second of 15 in another seller at York (6f, good) last Friday; recorded an RPR of 80 in those last two runs and he sets a good standard back in a novice.
runners.comment: RPR of 80 when winning and finishing second in sellers in last two runs; leading claims

runners.name: Harley
runners.spotlight: £22,000 half-brother three winners including Bomb Proof (5f including 2yo: RPR 95) and Beauty By My Side (7f 2yo/1m AW; 79); made a low-key start at Hamilton but he left that form behind when runner-up at Nottingham (6f, good) three weeks ago; should continue to improve with experience and he's respected.
runners.comment: Promising second at Nottingham last month and he's open to more progress; respected

runners.name: Claret Time
runners.spotlight: 5,000gns foal, £10,000 yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Dulla Bhatti (6f turf/1m AW; RPR 81), Modern World (French 1m1f) and Bhutto (French 1m); dam unraced half-sister to 7f-9.5f Group 3 winner Local Time, out of 1m2f Listed winner; hood is applied on debut and he could be one for longer trips later on.
runners.comment: Hood is applied on debut and he could be one for longer trips later on

runners.name: Callisterra
runners.spotlight: 11-1 and she finished a well-held fifth of ten at Thirsk (5f, good) last month; should know more this time but she needs plenty of improvement upped in trip.
runners.comment: Down the field at Thirsk last month and she needs plenty of improvement upped in trip

runners.name: Aodhan
runners.spotlight: 18,000gns yearling; seventh foal; gelded half-brother to winners Lady Roxby (5f including 2yo; RPR 89) and Lezardrieux (prolific 5f/6f including 2yo; 85); dam 5f 2yo winner (75); trainer/owner have a very promising 2yo on their hands in Adonius; the betting should be informative on debut.
runners.comment: Gelded half-brother to two sprint winners; betting should guide on debut

runners.name: Bear Lee
runners.spotlight: 10,000euros foal, 14,000euros yearling, 28,000gns breeze-up 2yo; sister to three winners including Gin O'Clock (7f/1m including 2yo; RPR 85) and Blakefell (7f; 75), half-sister to two other winners; dam 1m AW winner (86); has good target to aim at on debut and yard is 0-13 with 2yos this season.
runners.comment: Has good target to aim at on debut and yard is 0-13 with 2yos this season
runners.owner: N Bradley Racing 29, D Scott & P Spencer

runners.name: Golden Buzz
runners.spotlight: Big price and she finished a remote eighth of ten on Thirsk debut (5f, good) last month; passed over.
runners.comment: Big price and she finished a remote eighth of ten on Thirsk debut (5f, good) last month

runners.name: Tiger In The Tree
runners.spotlight: Dam once-raced (RPR 58) half-sister to a hurdle winner; well held at 40-1 here (6f, good) on her debut last month and same story when upped to 7f at Leicester last time; remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well held at 40-1 in both her runs (6f/7f) and remains best watched for now

runners.name: Mr Darling
runners.spotlight: Finished out the back in two 5f runs at Beverley (good to soft/good to firm) last month, with an RPR of 31 on both occasions.
runners.comment: Out the back in two 5f runs at Beverley last month, with an RPR of 31 on both occasions

runners.name: Wong Fei Hong
runners.spotlight: Weak 12-1 at Ripon (6f, good) on Thursday and he missed the break before finishing last of 11.
runners.comment: Weak 12-1 at Ripon (6f, good) on Thursday and he finished last of 11

races.race_name: racingtv.com/freetrial EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Undercover Affair
runners.spotlight: Has the odd quirk but he's been doing well in sellers, including a 5f Goodwood win on soft; led for a long way when fourth of 15 in 6f Rous at York (good) eight days ago; the return to 5f should suit; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Been in good form in sellers and the return to 5f should suit; thereabouts

runners.name: Champion Crown
runners.spotlight: 75,000euros breeze-up 2yo; half-brother to winners A Case Of You (5f-7f including 2yo/AW/Group 1; RPR 121), Ali Babes (6f; 73) and Loose Chippings (7f beach; 71); makes paper appeal for a stable among the 2yo winners; market confidence could prove significant.
runners.comment: Makes paper appeal so a market move could prove significant

runners.name: Quillan
runners.spotlight: £80,000 son of Kodi Bear and the first foal of a maiden half-sister to 10.6f Group 3 winner Bear Story; the betting should help guide to expectations.
runners.comment: £80,000 son of Kodi Bear; the betting should help guide to expectations
runners.owner: Mrs Anne Atkinson And H D Atkinson

runners.name: Rock Steady Beat
runners.spotlight: Showed up well for a long way on York Dante meeting debut and confirmed he possesses at least a fair level of ability when narrowly denied at Carlisle (5f, good to soft) 11 days ago, keeping on well once headed; remains open to progress and should go well.
runners.comment: Went close at Carlisle last week and bold show looks assured for top yard

runners.name: Cosmic Mystery
runners.spotlight: 220,000gns breeze-up buy; 4-1, promising 3l fourth of eight in Hilary Needler at Beverley (5f, good to firm) three weeks ago; likely to improve and go close at the least.
runners.comment: Promising start to her career when fourth at Beverley three weeks ago; should improve

runners.name: Shaniko
runners.spotlight: Last of 12 in seller at Chester (6f, good) on debut; left that run well behind when second of nine in novice at Nottingham (5f, good to soft) nine days ago but the form is only modest.
runners.comment: Left debut behind when second at Nottingham recently but the form is only modest

runners.name: Rubellite
runners.spotlight: Eight AW wins, including at Wolverhampton (1m6f) and Newcastle (1m4f) under Conor Whiteley on her last two outings; has to prove her effectiveness on turf (well held in three attempts much earlier in career) but has to be respected in her current mood.
runners.comment: On a hat-trick after two AW wins and good shout if showing she's as good on turf

runners.name: Stitching Wheel
runners.spotlight: Trainer not saddled a Flat winner in around six years; this maiden has tumbled down the handicap since joining from Andre Fabre but has been giving no indication she's ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: Tumbling in weights without suggesting she's likely to take advantage

runners.name: Himself
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row over 1m6f on AW early this year; hasn't been in the same form in two turf outings since returning from a break but is back on his last winning mark and Phil Kirby has tasted recent success in this race.
runners.comment: Three AW wins this year; below par back on turf last twice but down to last winning mark

runners.name: Hijo De La Luna
runners.spotlight: Won a classified race at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in February in a first-time visor; creditable fourth of ten in 1m2f Beverley handicap (good) on Tuesday, staying on again after getting outpaced; that suggests a return to further could suit but this is a big jump in trip.
runners.comment: Stayed on again when third over 1m2f at Beverley on Tuesday but this is a big jump in trip

runners.name: Tazaman
runners.spotlight: A dual winner (1m4f/2m1f, good to firm) in 2025; back to form with a run behind him when second of seven over 1m6f at Nottingham (good) three weeks ago; should go well.
runners.comment: Two fast-ground turf wins last year; good second at Nottingham latest; gets the vote

runners.name: Educate
runners.spotlight: 2-29, with both wins on AW; has been running no more than respectably on AW since joining Micky Hammond; first-time visor replaces cheekpieces back on turf.
runners.comment: Both wins on AW and running only respectably at best since joining this yard

runners.name: Mythical
runners.spotlight: Smart on the Flat in his prime but modest in recent times; has been pulled up in three hurdles since joining this yard; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Pulled up in three hurdles since joining this yard; leap of faith needed back on Flat

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV Free For 31 Days Handicap

runners.name: Here Forever
runners.spotlight: Landed 6f Ripon maiden (good) last September on his final run for George Boughey but didn't offer obvious short-term encouragement when down the field in 5f Thirsk handicap on return for new yard.
runners.comment: 6f maiden win last year but well held in 5f handicap on return for new yard

runners.name: Schrodinger's Cat
runners.spotlight: Ended his time with Harry Charlton with a C&D novice win (good) last September and solid placed efforts on three of four starts for new yard this season, including third of 13 at York (5f, good) eight days ago; this is less competitive and he's the one to beat.
runners.comment: In good form in big fields and this looks a bit easier; bold bid likely

runners.name: Atticum
runners.spotlight: Improved with each 2yo start, winning a Yarmouth novice (5f, good to soft) in October; respectable fifth in handicaps at Sandown (5f, good) and Windsor (6f, good to firm) this year; this represents a slight ease in grade; likely to go well.
runners.comment: 5f novice win at 2; two fair runs in handicaps and this is a slight ease in grade

runners.name: Lebron Power
runners.spotlight: Won three times as a 2yo (5f and 6f, soft and good to firm) but not so good in three runs this year.
runners.comment: Won three times last year but hasn't fired in three outings this season

runners.name: Dark Angel Star
runners.spotlight: Landed 5f Newcastle novice on third start and ended her juvenile campaign with a good second in 5f Chelmsford nursery; below par in 5f Wolverhampton handicap on reappearance in March and off for a further 12 weeks ahead of this return to the turf.
runners.comment: AW win last year but below par in Wolverhampton handicap on return; off 12 weeks since

runners.name: Wateen
runners.spotlight: Formerly trained by Alice Haynes; highly tried as a 2yo after winning a 5f Lingfield maiden (good), running her best race when third of seven in a Listed race at Deauville (5f, good to soft) last August; may have needed the outing after nine months off when weakening to finish sixth of seven in 5f Nottingham handicap (good) for new yard three weeks ago.
runners.comment: May have needed run on stable debut/reappearance but needed to see more

races.race_name: Start Your Racing TV Free Trial Handicap

runners.name: Far Above The Law
runners.spotlight: Has started off very well for Michael Herrington, landing a big-field handicap at Ascot (5f, good to firm) and going close in another at Thirsk (5f, good; Spring Is Sprung fourth, American Bay seventh) three weeks ago; a further 2lb nudge shouldn't prevent a prominent showing in his current mood, particularly with the promising Conor Whiteley taking 7lb off.
runners.comment: Arrives on back of a win at Ascot and second at Thirsk (both big fields); should go well

runners.name: Fidelius
runners.spotlight: Record of 6-17 on AW includes wins in February/March; not disgraced back on turf at Bath last month but below-par sixth at Goodwood since and now 0-10 on the grass.
runners.comment: 6-17 on AW but 0-10 on turf, including below-par sixth at Goodwood latest

runners.name: I'm Next
runners.spotlight: Showed career-best form when winning comfortably at Beverley (5f, good) in April; creditable in-frame efforts on two of his three outings since and ought to be in the mix again.
runners.comment: Beverley win in April and in frame on two of three runs since; ought to be in mix

runners.name: American Bay
runners.spotlight: First of two wins came in a novice over 6f here on good to firm; pair of placed efforts in 5f AW handicaps in March but hasn't quite matched that in two 5f handicaps at Thirsk (good/good to firm) since, with reopposing pair Far Above The Law and Spring Is Sprung ahead of him latterly; the first-time cheekpieces he wore last time are left off.
runners.comment: Course winner; respectable efforts of late but remains 3lb above his win mark.

runners.name: Alligator Alley
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a terrific spell on AW at the end of 2025, winning four times; given a break since a lesser effort behind Spring Is Sprung at Southwell in January; runner-up in this race last year but off 14lb lower.
runners.comment: Four AW wins at end of 2025 but off since lesser run in January; second in this last year
runners.owner: R J H Limited And Ruth Carr

runners.name: Adrestia
runners.spotlight: Won the Palace Of Holyroodhouse at 2025 Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm); also second in a Glorious Goodwood handicap and a Beverley Listed race later in 2025; last month's Windsor reappearance may have been needed and she's back on a good mark if returning to form in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Royal Ascot win last year; reappearance may have been needed; big player in new tongue-tie

runners.name: Night Storm
runners.spotlight: Returned from 15-month absence with 5f AW maiden/novice wins in November/December; advanced his form again when third of eight on 5f Newcastle handicap debut in January; remains with unexposed potential back from a further five months off; a heavy defeat on his 2024 Newmarket debut shouldn't be taken as evidence he won't prove effective on turf.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW last winter after absence; back on turf with unexposed potential

runners.name: Spring Is Sprung
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 8-35 includes five turf wins; creditable fourth (Far Above The Law second) of 17 at Thirsk (5f, good) three weeks ago; likely to be competitive again.
runners.comment: Not far behind Far Above The Law at Thirsk latest; likely to be competitive again

runners.name: Ziggy's Triton
runners.spotlight: Saw off 11 rivals when scoring at Musselburgh (5f, good; Class 4) in April; found out in much stronger company since but has dipped to only 1lb above the mark he defied at Musselburgh now back in slightly calmer waters; enters calculations.
runners.comment: Below par since Mussleburgh win in April but this is a slightly easier assignment

runners.name: Muker
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner, including this race on good to firm off 7lb higher in 2024 (sixth last year); quiet in three runs this season but worth a look in the betting given his previous in this contest.
runners.comment: C&D winner but quiet in three runs this year; could only consider if backed

runners.name: Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Unraced on ground softer than good; record of 2-6 since handicapping includes a narrow win over C&D on penultimate start; neck second to Ganthorpe at Carlisle latest; respected in current form.
runners.comment: Scored over C&D on penultimate start; neck second to Ganthorpe at Carlisle latest

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has form claims on her close third at Redcar (5f, good to firm) on penultimate outing; ran as if something was possibly amiss latest (wore visor); now goes in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has form claims on her close third at Redcar on penultimate start

runners.name: Ganthorpe
runners.spotlight: Below par in the C&D event won by Auspicious but reversed the placings at Carlisle (6f, good) later in May, beating him by neck and getting off the mark at the tenth attempt; may build on that win.
runners.comment: Off the mark in Carlisle event last time, reversing C&D placings with Auspicious

runners.name: Mereside Spark
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden; proved somewhat resurgent with fourth of six, beaten 4l, upped to 6f at Catterick (good to firm; bled from nose) most recently; may build on that effort.
runners.comment: Proved somewhat resurgent upped to 6f at Catterick last time

runners.name: Hares Bredth
runners.spotlight: Has inconsistent form but latest effort is perhaps best forgiven (something was seemingly amiss); ties in with Auspicious on C&D running earlier in May (best handicap effort).
runners.comment: Ties in with Auspicious on C&D running two starts ago (best handicap effort)

runners.name: Superstorm
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has broadly respectable 7f form this term; gives the impression this return to 6f is worth exploring; possibilities off current mark with Mikkel Mortensen taking off 5lb.
runners.comment: Gives impression this return to 6f is worth exploring; possibilities off current mark

runners.name: Northern Tempest
runners.spotlight: Looks capable of being involved but is proving an inconsistent maiden; modest fourth when favourite at Wetherby most recently, failing to back up his placed effort at Ripon (6f, good).
runners.comment: Capable of being involved but is proving an inconsistent maiden
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And M Dods

runners.name: Prima Domina
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid, having failed to back up her debut effort (6f, AW); possibly unsuited by the drop to 5f at Redcar (handicap debut) last time but has something to prove.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid, having failed to back up debut effort

runners.name: Charcon
runners.spotlight: Posted one of her better efforts returned to turf last time but still recorded only a meagre RPR; chance depends on whether first-time headgear prompts improvement.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the effects of first-time headgear

runners.name: Oasis Cover
runners.spotlight: Five-race maiden; best effort when running-on third at Nottingham (5f, good) on penultimate start and sole turf attempt; may show progress back in this sphere; one to consider.
runners.comment: Best effort when third at Nottingham on penultimate start and sole turf attempt
runners.owner: All About York Partnership And Partner

runners.name: Muddy Nora
runners.spotlight: Posted one of her better RPRs in the Carlisle event won by Ganthorpe but again finished out of the money and needs to improve; has the worst chance on bare figures.
runners.comment: Has the worst chance on bare figures

races.race_name: Watch Race Replays At racingtv.com "Confined" Handicap (No Win In 2026)

runners.name: Keats House
runners.spotlight: Developed into a consistent sort last term; unable to sustain the form in two outings this season but returning to Class 6 may prove beneficial; won his last attempt in this grade.
runners.comment: Return to Class 6 may prove beneficial; won his last attempt in this grade

runners.name: Okiru
runners.spotlight: Jumped path and unseated rider in sole attempt on turf and over 1m (penultimate start); raced over 7f on AW otherwise, running-on fifth last time; interesting off a reduced mark dropped in class.
