runners.comment: Not without a chance on his AW form; interesting off reduced mark dropped in class

runners.name: Eeetee
runners.spotlight: Needs to revive but he's interesting returned to this venue with Ripon reappearance under his belt; record of 4-9 at Redcar includes a success off current mark in this contest last year.
runners.comment: Record of 4-9 at Redcar includes a success off current mark in this contest last year
runners.owner: Peter Clarke Racing And John Wainwright

runners.name: Oscar's Sister
runners.spotlight: Gained sole success in 2022 but her record over 7f at Redcar features two short-head defeats last term and a respectable fourth in latest outing; in the mix, provided she's as effective back at 1m.
runners.comment: Gained sole win in 2022 but her Redcar record (over 7f) is broadly solid

runners.name: Volenti
runners.spotlight: Back in better form with fourth-place finishes the last twice; still has a better strike-rate on Newcastle AW than on turf but he's on an attractive mark and Conor Whiteley takes off a bonus 7lb; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Back in better form; on an attractive mark and Conor Whiteley takes off a bonus 7lb

runners.name: Soda
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who has shown ability over 6f/7f, last time still seeming green; bred to stay this new trip on handicap debut; may take a step forward and is one to consider.
runners.comment: Still seemed green last time; may take a step forward now handicapping over new trip

runners.name: Luminous Warrior
runners.spotlight: Not solid on his form since defying a 4lb higher mark on AW in December and has performed poorly in his two turf attempts for current yard; otherwise well treated if back on song.
runners.comment: Not solid on his form since defying a 4lb higher mark on AW in December

runners.name: Chuzzlewit
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-9 for current yard but he proved resurgent with close second at Newcastle (1m, AW) most recently and has possibilities provided that effort is backed up; peak mark was much higher.
runners.comment: Record of 0-9 for current yard but he proved resurgent with close second on AW last time

runners.name: Sparkling Pink
runners.spotlight: Appears inconsistent but her Class 6 form comprises a win over 6f for previous yard and placed effort over 7f on penultimate outing; 1m trip is the unknown returned to this grade.
runners.comment: Class 6 form comprises a win over 6f and placed effort over 7f; new trip

runners.name: Poppeye
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two winners for his yard; has shown two bits of promise over middle distances and latest effort suggests this drop to 1m may suit on handicap debut; open to improvement.
runners.comment: Latest effort suggests this drop to 1m may suit on handicap debut; open to improvement

runners.name: Whiskey Pete
runners.spotlight: Long time since his sole win and three duck eggs this term help to demonstrate a big doubt over his current form; chance of revival depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Long time since sole win; has a big doubt over current form; headgear fitted

runners.name: War And Love
runners.spotlight: Form dipped sharply in the last of three AW runs early last year for Sir Mark Prescott and sold for only 1,000gns in September; market may prove best guide on belated reappearance.
runners.comment: Form dipped sharply in the last of three AW runs early last year for Sir Mark Prescott

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden whose poor form continues; opposed.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden whose poor form continues

runners.name: Yafaarr
runners.spotlight: Still has a stronger record on AW than on turf and is 0-8 in this sphere; however, gained sole win off current mark and has made the frame in both Redcar attempts; could go well.
runners.comment: Gained his AW win off current mark; has made the frame in both Redcar attempts

runners.name: Zous Juice
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo maiden who trailed home last (had an irregular heartbeat) in sole start this year; otherwise has bits of encouraging form; check the betting for further guidance.
runners.comment: Lightly raced maiden; had an irregular heartbeat in sole start this year

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Cash Cove
runners.spotlight: New Bay filly who built on her debut when fourth of six in 6.5f maiden at Doncaster 30 days ago; she can take another step forward and is firmly in the picture.
runners.comment: Built on debut when fourth at Doncaster 30 days ago; firmly in the picture

runners.name: Heldobeldo
runners.spotlight: Kameko filly; fair form when runner-up in novices at Kempton (6f) and Musselburgh (7f) this summer; she holds solid form claims.
runners.comment: Runner-up in novices at Kempton and Musselburgh this summer; she holds solid form claims

runners.name: Aminaty
runners.spotlight: Mohaather gelding; dam, 1m4f AW winner (RPR 82), is a half-sister to 1m4f/1m5f winner El C Wells; in very good hands and rates a interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Mohaather gelding; in very good hands and rates an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Vegas Lights
runners.spotlight: 15-2, encouraging debut third of eight in maiden at Leicester (7f, good) a week ago; this Dandy Man filly is open to progress and needs considering.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut third at Leicester; open to progress and needs considering

runners.name: Gliding Martha
runners.spotlight: 18-1, shaped well when a strong-travelling second of eight in Lingfield maiden (7f, good to firm) on her debut three weeks ago; this daughter of Ghaiyyath is a player with better to come.
runners.comment: Shaped well when strong-travelling debut second at Lingfield; a player with more to come

runners.name: Minnal
runners.spotlight: Pinatubo filly; dam, unplaced over 7f-8.6f (RPR 52), is a half-sister to winners Princess Loulou (1m2f Listed; dam of French Oaks winner Nashwa) and Easy Target (useful 6f-1m1f); enters calculations on her first run, especially if the betting speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: Pinatubo filly; enters calculations on debut, especially if betting speaks in her favour

runners.name: Caravel
runners.spotlight: 20,000 euros foal, 44,000 guineas yearling; Minzaal filly; half-sister to four winners, notably Victory Tip (1m; RPR 91) and The Predictor (2m; RPR 88); dam unraced half-sister to smart 7f-10.5f winner Port Lions; not out of things on her debut, especially if the market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Minzaal filly; half-sister to four winners; not out of things on her debut

runners.name: Coral Cove
runners.spotlight: 25,000 euros foal, 19,000 euros yearling; sister to 7f AW winner Glitter And Glory (RPR 77); dam, 1m4f winner (RPR 88), is closely related to 6f winner Kodias Sangarius, out of 6f/1m winning half-sister to Group 3 winner Satchem (6f/1m); this Lucky Vega enters calculations, especially if the market speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: This Lucky Vega enters calculations, especially if the market speaks in her favour

runners.name: Gerry's Darling
runners.spotlight: 20,000 guineas yearling; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Concert (RPR 71); dam, unraced half-sister to winners Secret Weapon (HK 1m2f Group 2) and Code Red (6f/7f Listed), is out of a half-sister to 1m4f Group 3 winner Airmail Special; this Palace Pier filly is another debutante to consider.
runners.comment: This Palace Pier filly is another debutante to consider

runners.name: Star Martini
runners.spotlight: 10,000 euros yearling, resold 16,000 guineas yearling, 65,000 euros 2yo; half-sister to winners Enceladus (10.4f; RPR 79) and Moab (1m6f AW; 75); dam, prolific US 7f-8.5f winner (Grade 2; RPR 112); this daughter of Starspangledbanner appeals on paper and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: This daughter of Starspangledbanner appeals on paper and can't be ruled out

runners.name: China In Your Hand
runners.spotlight: Cracksman filly; half-sister to 6f winner Territorial Knight (RPR 92); dam, unraced sister to 7f 2yo winner Ibiza Rocks, is a half-sister to 1m-1m2f Flat and 2m hurdle winner I Still Have Faith; a considered newcomer.
runners.comment: Cracksman filly; a considered newcomer
runners.owner: C Wright Mrs C Corbett G Hargreave

runners.name: Pride's Queen
runners.spotlight: 17-2, eighth of 12 in maiden at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on her debut four weeks ago; this Territories filly can take a step forward, especially over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Eighth at Windsor on her debut; can take a step forward, especially over this longer trip

runners.name: Dun Eideann
runners.spotlight: Territories filly; never dangerous on both her starts, still green when sixth in 6f AW novice at Lingfield 16 days ago; she's open to progress.
runners.comment: Still green when sixth in 6f AW novice at Lingfield 16 days ago; she's open to progress

runners.name: Serenity Bay
runners.spotlight: 16-1 and very green when beating one in maiden at Windsor (6f, good) on her debut last month; this Bayside Boy filly can take a step forward.
runners.comment: Very green when beating one at Windsor on her debut last month; can take a step forward

races.race_name: Summer Saturday Series At Doncaster Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Seven Sisters
runners.spotlight: Kingman filly who is a half-sister to the high-class Barney Roy; has shaped with plenty of promise when placed on both her starts, still green when a running-on third of 11 in 8.5f Wolverhampton maiden on latter occasion; the one to beat with this step up in trip also a likely plus.
runners.comment: Still green when third in 8.5f Wolverhampton maiden last time; the one to beat

runners.name: Chimes Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start for William Haggas (trained debut by David Menuisier) when runner-up in 1m Haydock novice but failed to build on it with fifth in 1m Windsor maiden last month.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Haydock but failed to build on it with Windsor fifth

runners.name: Hollywell Stream
runners.spotlight: Shaped well when a debut second in 7f novice here in October but (evens) came in last of six on her return in 8.5f Wolverhampton novice 22 days ago; not written off.
runners.comment: Last of six on her return in Wolverhampton novice 22 days ago; needs to get back on track

runners.name: Khepri
runners.spotlight: War Front filly; half-sister to US Graded stakes-placed 1m dirt 2yo winner Real Men Violin; dam twice-raced half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 1 winner I Want Revenge; in very good hands so this newcomer needs considering.
runners.comment: War Front filly; in very good hands so this newcomer needs considering

runners.name: Toastmaster
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but he built up a solid record for Simon and Ed Crisford and has shaped well on both runs for his new yard when a close third in handicaps at York (7f) and over C&D; can race off the same mark here so another bold showing is on the cards.
runners.comment: Shaped well both runs for new yard when third at York and over C&D; bold showing on cards
runners.owner: Follow The Majer Partners & S Herrington

runners.name: French Affair
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f winner at 2yrs; yet to add to his tally this season but has advanced his form, runner-up at York before a good C&D fourth last time out; firmly in the picture again.
runners.comment: In very good form, runner-up at York before C&D fourth; firmly in the picture again

runners.name: Mighty Vega
runners.spotlight: Kept busy in 2026 and has bagged a pair of 7f handicaps at Lingfield (AW and turf); posted another solid effort with third of seven at Chepstow (7f) and he enters calculations once more.
runners.comment: Dual 7f winner; another solid effort with third at Chepstow; enters calculations again

runners.name: Flight Control
runners.spotlight: Went in at 80-1 on his debut in 6f novice here in April and not disgraced both runs since, fifth of 11 in C&D novice on latter occasion; goes into handicaps now and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: 80-1 debut winner here and not disgraced both runs since; not ruled out on handicap debut

runners.name: Comprador
runners.spotlight: Showed some fair form without winning for Ed Walker and started out for his new yard with a solid third of 11 in 7f Wolverhampton handicap 22 days ago; a bit more is needed though if he's to gain a first win here; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Solid Wolverhampton third for new yard latest; bit more needed though to gain a first win

runners.name: Cotai Eye Joe
runners.spotlight: Looked on the up when landing 7f Wolverhampton maiden in April but he came in just 12th on his handicap debut at York (7f) four weeks ago; not written off yet though after just four runs.
runners.comment: Landed Wolverhampton maiden but just 12th on York h'cap debut; not written off yet though
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing & Alfa Site Services

runners.name: Watcha Snoop
runners.spotlight: Landed 6f Ascot novice in September but there's not been much to enthuse about since and he came in last of 13 in 7f Kempton handicap 24 days ago; cheekpieces are reached for now.
runners.comment: Landed 6f Ascot novice in September but there's not been much to enthuse about since

runners.name: Vega Storm
runners.spotlight: Lucky Vega gelding who took a step forward on his third run with fifth of 11 in 7f Catterick novice last month; should have more to offer now stepping into handicap company so needs considering.
runners.comment: Step forward with Catterick fifth last month; should have more to offer on handicap debut

runners.name: Three Non Blondes
runners.spotlight: Made a winning start in handicaps at Wolverhampton (7f) in January and not disgraced with fifth of 14 at Newcastle (6f) when last seen in April for Karl Burke; since changed hands for 45,000gns and not out of things back up to 7f for her new yard.
runners.comment: Not disgraced with fifth at Newcastle for Karl Burke; not out of things for her new yard

runners.name: Gesayed
runners.spotlight: Debut 6f novice winner at Hamilton last June; off ten months after but she's performed with credit on both runs this spring, fifth of seven in 1m Ascot novice last month; likely better to come now going handicapping.
runners.comment: Solid fifth in 1m Ascot novice last month; likely better to come now handicapping

runners.name: Galileo Island
runners.spotlight: Scored twice over 7f last summer and continued in good form in AW handicaps in the winter; back from a 170-day layoff here though (cheekpieces added) so needs to hit the ground running.
runners.comment: In good form on AW this winter; off for 170 days though so needs to hit ground running

runners.name: Harswell Angel
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f juvenile winner; arrives in good nick, not enjoying the best of runs when eighth of 20 in 7f York handicap four weeks ago; possibilities off an easing mark.
runners.comment: Arrives in good nick, not enjoying best of runs when eighth at York latest; possibilities

runners.name: Chale Chalo
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with 7f Lingfield win and backed it up with a good second in 7f Southwell handicap on New Year's Day; absent since though so has his fitness to prove.
runners.comment: Good second at Southwell on New Year's Day; absent since though so has fitness to prove

runners.name: Talis Evolvere
runners.spotlight: Started 2026 with victory at Southwell (1m) and right back to form of late, runner-up in 1m Goodwood handicap eight days ago; he's weighted to go very well off the same mark.
runners.comment: Very good runner-up in Goodwood h'cap eight days ago; weighted to go well off same mark

runners.name: Walson's Law
runners.spotlight: A two-time 1m/7f scorer last summer; lightly raced since and failed to build on solid reappearance Ascot third when only tenth in 7f Goodwood handicap last month; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Only tenth in 7f Goodwood handicap last month; needs to bounce back
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Lady Cobham

runners.name: Monsieur Bondy
runners.spotlight: Landed a pair of 7f novices at Redcar and Newcastle last summer but he's failed to kick on sent handicapping, beating one on his seasonal return over C&D two weeks ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Landed 7f novices at Redcar and Newcastle in 2025 but failed to kick on sent handicapping

runners.name: Leadman
runners.spotlight: Shaped very well when second in a big-field York handicap (7f) in May but he's twice failed to build on it, just a fair sixth back there (7f) eight days ago; handily weighted but it's now 13 runs since his last victory.
runners.comment: Shaped very well when second in York handicap in May but he's twice failed to build on it

runners.name: Goldmoyne
runners.spotlight: Completed a four-time on the AW at Lingfield (1m) in March; has continued in good nick on turf, fourth of 11 in 7.5f Beverley handicap three weeks ago; ought to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Good fourth of 11 in 7.5f Beverley handicap three weeks ago; ought to be thereabouts

runners.name: Eldrickjones
runners.spotlight: Scored at Newcastle (7f) in December and largely in good form after on AW; only 13th on rare turf start in 1m York handicap last time though so needs to take a step forward.
runners.comment: In good on AW but 13th on rare turf start at York last time; needs to take a step forward

runners.name: The Green Man
runners.spotlight: A three-time 6f/7f scorer in 2025; kept busy and in good form (on the AW) this term until only 16th in 7f York handicap eight days ago; he's the type to bounce back though.
runners.comment: In good form until 16th in York h'cap eight days ago; he's the type to bounce back though
runners.owner: The Dalby Family And P Kirby

runners.name: Mudamer
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f Catterick scorer in October; back in very good form this season, and probably best forgiven his latest 11th in 7f Thirsk handicap when missing the start and unable to lead; the sort to bounce back.
runners.comment: Best forgiven latest 11th at Thirsk when unable to lead; the sort to bounce back

runners.name: Diligent Resdev
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f/7f scorer in 2024; quite lightly raced since but back to form with sixth of 11 in 7.5f Beverley handicap three weeks ago; not out of things with this shorter trip a plus.
runners.comment: Back to form with sixth at Beverley latest; not out of things with shorter trip a plus

runners.name: Obelix
runners.spotlight: Resumed winning ways in 14-runner handicap at Wetherby (7f) 16 days ago; up 5lb but scored cosily there so this strong traveller is a likely player again for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: Cosily scored at Wetherby latest; up 5lb but a likely player again for his in-form yard
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Master Richard
runners.spotlight: A dual 7f winner in 2025 for Ann Duffield; returned with very good placed efforts in 7f handicaps at Newcastle and Musselburgh this spring and not out of things starting out for another new yard here.
runners.comment: Very good third at Newcastle and Musselburgh this spring; not out of things for new yard
runners.owner: T S Ingham & Mrs Liz Ingham

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App Handicap

runners.name: Woolridge
runners.spotlight: Is 0-11 but he posted his best effort for Scott Dixon when third of 12 in 6f Newcastle handicap last month; not ruled out back on turf.
runners.comment: Is 0-11 but best effort for Scott Dixon when third at Newcastle last month; not ruled out

runners.name: Ran Amok
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last July and he signalled he is ready to go in again on the back of breathing surgery when a very good head second of ten in 6f Chester handicap a week ago; merits serious consideration nudged up 1lb.
runners.comment: C&D scorer; signalled ready to go in with very good Chester second; holds very good claims

runners.name: Fizzy Cristal
runners.spotlight: Doubled her tally with 6f handicap success at Newcastle in may and backed it up with a solid fourth of 11 at Chester (6f) three weeks ago; she's less exposed than most of these and in the mix.
runners.comment: Solid fourth at Chester latest; she's less exposed than most of these and in the mix

runners.name: Yachtsman
runners.spotlight: Took his form up a notch when gaining a breakthrough victory in 6f Windsor handicap; not discredited off a 6lb higher mark when a close fourth of five at Catterick (6f) 30 days ago; not out of things now eased 1lb.
runners.comment: Won at Windsor handicap; not discredited with Catterick fourth since; not out of things

runners.name: Men Of Honour
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f/7f maiden for Andrew Slattery in 2025; yet to hit top form for his new stable, though not best drawn when beating one in 6f Ripon handicap 17 days ago; no surprise to see him take a step forward here from a falling mark.
runners.comment: Not best drawn when beating one at Ripon latest; no surprise to see him take step forward
runners.owner: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Sugar Baby
runners.spotlight: A four-time winner in 2025 (including over C&D) and he comes here on the back of an encouraging fifth of 11 in 6f Thirsk handicap four days ago; a likely player off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: C&D winner; encouraging fifth at Thirsk four days ago; likely player off the same mark
runners.owner: Hard Held Partnership And Angus Racing

runners.name: Lumenbourg
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways in 6f Carlisle handicap 11 days ago, cosily making all; up 4lb but this dual course scorer must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Back to winning ways at Carlisle 11 days ago; this dual course scorer enters calculations
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Great
runners.spotlight: A fair 6f winner for Richard Hannon in 2025; yet to reach that form in five runs for Sam England though, again slowly into his stride when eighth of ten in 6f handicap at Pontefract 12 days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Only eighth of ten in 6f handicap at Pontefract 12 days ago; others are preferred

runners.name: Dark Side Thunder
runners.spotlight: A dual 6f scorer last season; comes here on the back of a good third of eight in 6f Leicester handicap last month so needs considering.
runners.comment: Comes here on the back of a good third of eight at Leicester last month; considered

runners.name: Another Investment
runners.spotlight: Ended a long losing run over C&D last June but there's been little to enthuse about since, tenth in 6f Ripon handicap in April; needs to take a big step forward after a break.
runners.comment: Ended a long losing run over C&D last June but there's been little to enthuse about since

runners.name: Oasis Sunrise
runners.spotlight: Is enjoying an excellent season and bids for a hat-trick after back-to-back handicap wins at Chepstow (1m4f) and Leicester (1m2f) recently; up 4lb but big shout with promising 5lb claimer Ashley Lewis up.
runners.comment: Bids for hat-trick after wins at Chepstow and Leicester; big shout despite 4lb rise

runners.name: Trojan Truth
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good summer in 2025 when bagging a hat-trick; comes here on the back of a good second of 8 to Oasis Sunrise at Chepstow (1m4f) last time so must enter calculations.
runners.comment: Good second of 8 to Oasis Sunrise at Chepstow (1m4f) last time so must enter calculations

runners.name: Kurios Lady
runners.spotlight: Reliable maiden and not discredited despite beating one when fifth in 1m4f Leicester handicap last month, set plenty to do in a steadily-run race; not out of things for her in-form yard.
runners.comment: Not discredited when fifth at Leicester last month; not out of things for her in-form yard

runners.name: Opera Wave
runners.spotlight: Gained a first win in 1m3f Southwell handicap in September but yet to build on that, last of 3 in 11.5f Windsor handicap 26 days ago; more is required if she's to double her tally on turf.
runners.comment: Last of 3 at Windsor 26 days ago; more is required if she's to double her tally on turf

runners.name: Nanny Park
runners.spotlight: Winless since 2024 and she hasn't offered much in three starts for Sara Ender, seventh of 11 at Pontefract (1m2f) 12 days ago; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Winless since 2024 and she hasn't offered much in three starts for Sara Ender

runners.name: Bulldog Spirit
runners.spotlight: A dual winner in 2025 but he's been below par in a trio of runs this season and failed to beat a rival at Nottingham (1m2f) last time; retried in headgear here.
runners.comment: A dual winner in 2025 but he's been below par in a trio of runs this season

runners.name: Pleasant Man
runners.spotlight: On a long losing run but he comes here in good nick, third of 11 in 9.5f Wolverhampton handicap four days ago; his best recent form is on the AW though.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but he comes here in good nick; best recent form is on AW though

runners.name: My Ballyquinn
runners.spotlight: Handicap debut at Hamilton (1m3f, good) three weeks ago came in just a three-runner race but he came through late in the day to deny a respected rival who won starts either side; he'd also shown plenty of promise before that and 4lb rise could seriously underestimate his potential.
runners.comment: Won a three-runner race last time but 4lb rise could seriously underestimate his potential

runners.name: Central Command
runners.spotlight: Visored this term; won 1m2f AW handicaps at Newcastle in May and Lingfield 16 days ago; 1m4f looks worth a go (despite pedigree) but he was very disappointing at Haydock (good to soft) in between in his only turf race.
runners.comment: Won two 1m2f AW handicaps but very disappointing on turf debut (good to soft) in between

runners.name: Asia Force
runners.spotlight: Consistent form when runner-up in all three novice events and third of nine on handicap debut at Chester (1m2f, good to soft); unraced on firmer than good; rallied last time as if 1m4f could help but he does need to find extra.
runners.comment: Consistent form and 1m4f in this second handicap could help but he does need to find extra

runners.name: Mudita
runners.spotlight: Second start was a five-runner maiden win at Ripon (1m2f, good) in April; while she kept on in a handicap at Nottingham (1m2f, good) 17 days ago, despite hanging left, she was never dangerous and managed just fifth of six; overall improvement is needed.
runners.comment: Hung left when never dangerous on handicap debut; overall improvement is needed

runners.name: Cranachan
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck at Kempton (1m4f, AW) on reappearance in March and went one better when 4-5 in a five-runner maiden at Catterick (1m4f, good) in April; needed plenty of riding in the latter but finished well on top and the runner-up has won since; it looks like he'll stay further but he's also of interest on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Needed some riding but finished well on top when 4-5 in a 1m4f Catterick maiden last time

runners.name: Home Secretary
runners.spotlight: Beat a total of one rival last autumn in four starts over 7f/1m on AW and softer than good; gelded since and makes handicap debut over much further in first cheekpieces after 236 days off; 2lb wrong but, from this yard, you know he could reveal himself in a new light; market should be revealing.
runners.comment: From this yard, he could reveal himself in a new light; market should be revealing

races.race_name: Dine In Sherwoods Restaurant Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Cyrano De Bergerac
runners.spotlight: 150,000gns yearling by Palace Pier; promising second behind a 1-2 shot on Lingfield debut (1m2f, AW) in April and he built on that when going close in a Windsor novice (1m2f, good to soft) last Monday; sets a good standard on that form and he has fast-ground winners on dam's side; should be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Went close at Windsor last week and he sets good standard on that form; strong contender

runners.name: Seacole
runners.spotlight: 400,000gns yearling; third foal; sister to 1m 2yo winner Seacruiser (RPR 104), half-sister to Spanish 7.5f-1m4f winner Velerin; dam 6f-1m2f maiden (67), half-sister to 1m4f Group 2 winners Across The Stars and Bronze Cannon, out of useful 7f winner; has a striking pedigree and she needs watching in market on debut.
runners.comment: 400,000gns yearling; has a striking pedigree and she needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Flag Of Eva
runners.spotlight: Big prices and he's finished tailed off here (8.3f, good) and at Kempton (1m3f, AW) in his two runs this spring; remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: Big prices and he's finished tailed off in his two runs this spring

races.race_name: Racedays For £9 At Nottingham Racecourse Training Series Apprentice Handicap (RE Series)

runners.name: Leadenhall
runners.spotlight: Recorded his fourth win when scoring over C&D (good) last month and he backed that up with a creditable sixth in a big field at York (1m1f, good); remains feasibly treated on his best form and he's in the mix back at this track.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last month before a good run in a big field at York; in the mix

runners.name: Equion
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner who had a near-miss off this mark at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December; didn't fire in his final two runs during the winter but has claims if he can rediscover his spark after a break.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner and he has claims if he can rediscover his spark after a break

runners.name: Ramli
runners.spotlight: Shadwell castoff, by Frankel; finished third in a Huntingdon bumper (2m, good) on debut and he made it third-time lucky on the Flat when scoring at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) this month; looks on workable mark now switched to a handicap and he should have more to offer; respected.
runners.comment: Won a Wolverhampton maiden and he's open to more progress on handicap debut; respected

runners.name: Zowal
runners.spotlight: Encouraging reappearance run in the Spring Mile at Doncaster and he built on that to win at Pontefract (1m, good to soft); that was a narrow success but he beat five subsequent winners in that race and is only 2lb higher here; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Only 2lb higher than for Pontefract win and he beat five subsequent winners in that race

runners.name: Lord Normandy
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who trounced two rivals in a Newmarket handicap (1m2f, good) on his final run last season; 6lb higher on his return but he still has potential and should be fine on this drop back in trip; needs a close look in the market.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo who won at Newmarket on his final run last season; respected on return

races.race_name: Wildwest Beer Festival 4th July Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Time Loop
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo; well held at Kempton last month but 1m4f may have stretched her and she was a creditable fourth on handicap debut at Newcastle (1m2f) in January; looks interesting back at this trip and it wouldn't be a surprise if she resumed her progress.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 5yo and she could resume her progress back at this trip; interesting

runners.name: Spoken Truth
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 5yo; overall record of 1-13 but he's been in good form for new yard this season and has been placed in Class 2 events (1m2f) in his last two starts; seems versatile ground-wise and he could make a major impact if this sets up for his closing style.
runners.comment: Placed in Class 2 events in last two starts and he's respected on this drop back in grade

runners.name: Green Triangle
runners.spotlight: Won twice in Ireland for Joseph O'Brien; some creditable efforts for current yard but his style can depend on luck and he's finished a well-held eighth at Newbury (1m) in last two starts; mark continues to fall but he needs to raise his game back up in trip; blinkers return and cheekpieces are removed.
runners.comment: His style can depend on luck and he needs to raise his game back up in trip

runners.name: Bella's Path
runners.spotlight: Hooded on all three runs last season for Paul Attwater and looked promising in victory and defeat (all at 1m); this is a big ask on handicap debut after eight months off, but opening mark looks fair and she's bred to stay this new trip; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer and she needs watching in the market after eight months off

runners.name: Model Yuko
runners.spotlight: Al Kazeem filly who was an easy winner of a Wolverhampton maiden (8.6f) last June; close third on handicap debut at Goodwood (1m2f, good) next time and she had tough task in a French Listed race (very soft) in final run last term; still unexposed after only five starts and she could kick on again back in handicaps this season; needs a close look on return.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo and she could kick on again back in handicaps this season; needs close look

runners.name: Towerlands
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on AW; finished a respectable fourth back on turf at Lingfield (1m2f, good to firm) last month but was never dangerous in that race and others are preferred.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on AW; fair fourth back on turf latest but others are preferred

runners.name: Nolton Cross
runners.spotlight: Six-time AW winner but he's 0-11 on turf and has struggled at Newmarket (1m4f, good to firm) and Hamilton (1m5f, good) in last two runs; now drops back into a Class 4 event but he has plenty to prove back at this trip.
runners.comment: 0-11 on turf and he's struggled back in this sphere in his last two starts

runners.name: Organ
runners.spotlight: All 12 runs have been on turf including three consecutive wins over 7f/1m (good/good to firm) in May and June of last year; right out of form in final three runs last season but he's got back on track this spring and was only beaten a nose at Chester (10.3f) last month; nudged up 2lb but he was clear of the third last time and is still unexposed at this trip; big player.
runners.comment: Went very close at Chester (10.3f) and he's a big player again off 2lb higher

races.race_name: It's Racing Staff Week Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Desert Treasure
runners.spotlight: Promising win on debut at Yarmouth (5f, good) last July and followed up at 1-4 in Windsor novice ten days later; finished down the field when 11-2 favourite on handicap debut at York last month but she may have needed that run and she still has potential for top yard; respected.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly who is 2-3 and she may have needed her reappearance run at York; respected

runners.name: Palmeira
runners.spotlight: Hold-up performer who is 0-13 but she's run creditably at big prices in three Listed events (5f, good) this season; now makes her belated handicap debut and has possibilities if she gets a good pace and some luck.
runners.comment: 0-13 but she's run well in three Listed events this season; not ruled out

runners.name: Paradise Walk
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at fifth attempt when battling well to beat her main market rival in four-runner maiden at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month; this is tougher back in a handicap but she's still unexposed and has shaped as though this drop to 5f could suit; respected.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo who won a Windsor maiden last time and she's respected back in a handicap

runners.name: Sugar Hill Babe
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a close second at Musselburgh in April and she built on that when forging clear over C&D (good) last month; up 6lb but she has form figures of 3211 at this track and was as good as ever last time; big player.
runners.comment: Made it 2-4 at Nottingham when forging clear over C&D last time; big player

runners.name: Tiva
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been at 5f on turf (good/good to firm) and she's been beaten less than 2l in her last two starts; looks weighted near best off this mark but she should go well again.
runners.comment: Looks weighted near best off this mark but she's in form and should go well again

runners.name: Secret Mistral
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner including four on turf (good/soft); runner-up in final two starts last season but she struggled at Wolverhampton (6f) in her final run last year; this drop back in trip looks a good move on her return but her last three wins have been in Class 5 events and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Six-time winner but last three have been in Class 5 events; has some work to do on return

runners.name: Elegant Erin
runners.spotlight: Eight-time turf winner including on fast and soft ground; ended last season with a good fourth in a Listed race at Ascot (5f) but she's been out of sorts in her four runs this year; on dangerous mark but she needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but she's been out of sorts this year and needs to get back on track

runners.name: Winchurch
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) in April and he got back on the scoresheet when rallying well for a clearcut success at Catterick (5f, good to soft) last week; 5lb higher here but this return to fast ground is no problem and he's respected back up in trip.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in April and he scored over 5f at Catterick last week; respected
runners.owner: SBK Racing & B & J Parr Racing Ltd

runners.name: Ingleby Archie
runners.spotlight: Four-time sprint winner who acts on any ground; his last success was 13 months ago but he had a near-miss behind a course specialist at Hamilton (6f, good to soft) last week; only 1lb higher here and he should make another bold bid.
runners.comment: Had a near-miss at Hamilton last week and he's only 1lb higher here; shortlisted

runners.name: Cairdeas
runners.spotlight: Reappeared with a good second at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) last month but he's not easy to predict and was a well-held fifth at Ripon last time; that took his record to 1-16 and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: Record of 1-16 and he was disappointing at Ripon last time; others are more convincing

runners.name: Early Release
runners.spotlight: Added to his three AW wins when making all at Bath (5.7f, firm) three weeks ago; that was a five-runner race but he was unchallenged and a 3lb rise looks fair enough; in the mix.
runners.comment: Made all at Bath (5.7f, firm) three weeks ago and a 3lb rise looks fair enough; in the mix

runners.name: Holbache
runners.spotlight: Admirable 8yo who recorded his 11th win when surging clear on his reappearance at Catterick (6f, good to firm) last month; has a 10lb rise to deal with but he still looks on a workable mark and his last four wins have come under Tom Kiely-Marshall; key player.
runners.comment: Recorded his 11th win when surging clear at Catterick; up 10lb but he's a key player

runners.name: Raffles Angel
runners.spotlight: Made it 4-8 on turf when scoring at Haydock (6f, good) last July but she's lost her way in three subsequent runs; had an excuse on soft ground on her Lingfield reappearance two weeks ago but this is competitive and she needs a big turnaround.
runners.comment: Record of 4-10 on turf but she's lost her way in last three starts; needs a big turnaround

races.race_name: Nottingham Racecourse Supporting Racing Staff Week Handicap

runners.name: Kalokalo
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 214231 in handicaps and he justified 6-4 favouritism when beating a clear second at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) 19 days ago; hung badly left in that race but a 6lb rise looks fair enough and he's open to more progress at this trip; key player.
runners.comment: Hung left at Windsor but he made it 2-6 in handicaps with that success; respected up 6lb

runners.name: Mr Bollinger
runners.spotlight: $85,000 yearling; half-brother to two winners including Exude (Canadian 1m3f turf); well held in novice/maiden events this spring but he has shown ability behind some useful prospects and he's a possible improver now switched to a handicap; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer and he's a possible improver with cheekpieces added back up in trip

runners.name: Miss Pretty
runners.spotlight: 9-1 winner on Kempton debut (7f, AW) last August but she's been well held in both subsequent runs including on handicap debut at Salisbury (1m2f, good; 12-1) last month; that was after an absence but she was always in rear and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Won on Kempton debut (7f) last August but she's been well held in both runs since

runners.name: Astracornus
runners.spotlight: No closer than sixth in his three qualifying runs but he was only beaten around 4l when joint-favourite in a Leicester novice (1m2f, good to firm) last month; should have more to offer now switched to handicaps and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Promising effort at Leicester last month and he needs a close look on handicap debut

runners.name: Distorted Truth
runners.spotlight: Camelot filly who is out of a useful 1m/1m2f winner (RPR 91); promising second on Kempton debut (1m3f, AW) in January but she's been beaten 31l and 72l in both subsequent runs; still early days but she needs a transformation on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Tailed off in her last two qualifying runs and needs a transformation on handicap debut
